The women seem to know all the people "worth while"; the men either become im- portant themselves or are surround~- ed with those who have "arrived".

These people are always posi- tive and self-assured. They never give ground or yield an inch in an argument. 6's (Displacement Card) are fixed and may develop fixations instead of the higher wisdom--depending entirely upon how it is handled by the individ- ual. The inclination of the 2 D is toward fixation and the major- ity are in danger of becoming so satisfied with what they do and how they think that they for- get to work with the principles that give the key to their high- est attainment. The men are excellent traders and merchants. If they follow an artistic line they are less apt to give evidence of their inher- ent power and often end by drift- ing or being content with a me- diocre performance. This is not so true of the women. All need a definite goal in the life-- a positive form of expression that will bring out their true worth and allow them to use it for uni- versal benefit. B Se: e for 2D. Merc. Irresolution in early life. JS If interfered with at home,

apt to leave; prefer adven- ture and uncertainty to res- traint. Love travel and seek foreign countries and people. Tf held to home because of duty and responsibility, will seek a compromise, Will not wholly evade responsibility but resent too mich of it. This is one of the "Fixed Principles" cards. When asso- ciated with the Venus influ- ence, these people will stick to a bad bargain or an unfor- tunate marriage “on principle" Often found as club leaders-~ partly social, partly for re- form and improvement. May be drawn to the church as a pro= fession or become active in church interests. Need a good education for success. Make excellent teachers and coun- selors, Civic minded and pub- lic spirited. Usually very well informed. Have a "salon". (The rest of the Sequence is in the Jupiter Line). In terms of the law of Karma, this is a "settlement" card, If strict honesty is adhered to, any financial restriction can always be worked out. If they become careless about ob- ligations, protection is lost. There is an innate fear (2)

of debt, so the wise ones are slow to incur obligations they cannot easily meet. They some~ times delay taking the initia- tive until an opportunity is lost. Usually some difficulty with finances at various pe- riods of the life. Benefits accrue from associa~ tions with established people or businesses. Can become very successful if risks and specu- lation are avoided, Are usual- ly satisfied with what they do and are well paid. Not given to business changes, once they have found a satisfactory occu- pation. This is an excellent card un= der Saturn and assures protec- tion and loving care in any illness, Not apt to have prob=- lems involving the illness of others; if they do, no burden is felt because service is giv- en in the name of love. Have positive and definite goals. Demanding and possessive. Want home as a center. Like to have working groups there. Much interested in young people and their education, Enthu- siastic about Parent-Teach-~ ers associations, Scout Clubs, work for underprivileged chil- dren, etc. Make good teach- ers--kindergarten, primary grades especially. Good or- ganizers and financiers, bankers. Can always choose the right vocation if allow the intuition to work, Can make considerable money, save for a while and then spend extravagantly. Generous spon- sors of art but more inter-~ ested in a productive busin- ess than art as a career.

May encounter opposition in early life, but succeed by their own merits later on. Attracted to art, music and beauty in all forms. Have interest and curiosity about things and people. Do not like responsibility and will not assume it unless obliged to. Like things to move rap- idly. Usually assured of suc- cess after 36, but may arrive much earlier. Make friends easily. Are clever in discern- ing the motives of others. Have good judgment because of mental keenness and are usu- ally good money makers. Do better with a professional career than in business. Make good lawyers, pleaders, writers, speakers. Can buy or sell jewelry or other ar- ticles of adornment. Make good interior decorators. If they choose business, it is well to cultivate a tal- ent as a side issue.

Want money but have to work for it. 13 is a testing num- ber and those who have not de- veloped a strong character may crumple under it; if they do, are consumed with self-pity. The life is not easy, but when problems are met squarely and strongly, great success may ensue. Should follow a line they feel best adapted for and not be forced into an unconge- nial occupation. Often become outstanding educators or reli- gious leaders. May have inter- ference from some male member of the family. Interested in the ground and gardens. Can succeed in foods and home com- modities. Should cultivate tolerance and unselfishness. Ruler- The Sun.

This date is slated for suc- cess--usually in a profess- ional line. Excellent promot- ers of the line in which they are most interested. Often at- tracted to the stage or plat~- form--musicians, composers, movie or stage actors. May be outstanding as statesmen or government officials, Must al- ways dominate and will not be dictated to. Are nervous; en- ergies and interests often fluctuating, but can some- times make a success of two lines of activity pursued at the same time. Should always have an important hobby to give an outlet for their dual- ity of interest. Have high standards of per-~ fection--for selves and oth- ers. May become over-meticu- lous and fussy. Power is in- creased by broadening outlook and humanitarian interests. Tendency to be self-centered, Good lawyers, teachers, lin- guists. The task of the 2 S is to live on a strictly constructive basis; to avoid self-indulgence at the expense of work or duty; to be willing to serve--regard- less of remmeration and not to spare themselves in hard work-- which is one phase of the mission they have come to fulfil.

Practicality and common sense must be early instilled. Under the strong Neptune influence of their Planetary Sequence (as far as the Saturn Card) they can be easily thrown off balance, lost in day- dreaming and a realm of unreality and later in life develop a neu- rosis that might have serious con- sequences. So great is the power, those who are deliberately headed for evil become the so-called "Black Magicians". (Mars-Neptune-Uranus). The "True Place" of the 2 S$ is the Uranus Card in the Uranus Line. Intuitional force in the highest degree is at their dis- posal-~but it requires an ear- nest desire plus steady applica- tion to establish a clear chan- nel. Those who are able to bring to their conscious minds their subconscious knowledge of Who They Are, know the right direc- tion to take and the right an- swers to their problems. In practical affairs, 2 S peo- ple make good buyers and traders; have an interest in foreign pla- ces and people and become success~ ful foreign agents and negotia-~ tors. There is a fine balance to be sought between spiritual ideals and sound business tactics. With the right cooperation (and all 2's need it) this can be--and of- ten is found.

There is a tendency to wilful- ness--with consequent self-undo- ing. They want their own way and often mislead themselves by their adherence to it. The women should strive to know the men with whom they are closely associated. They may eas- ily become a burden and a problem. The majority of the 2 S like diversion and good company. They are genial and popular~-easy drinkers and lively entertainers. Although, like all 2's, they want home roots, they are often obliged to work away from home. Circum- stances drive them to stand on their own feet--usually the hard way. When they do and make a suc- cess of it, they develop a strength of character that helps them to overcome their material obstacles. Merc. Power is gained through 8H friendships. The more friends

they have, the less are they subject to confusion. Are very dependent upon others and strive to keep their as- sociations a long time, It will be noted that the 8 H displaces the 7 S in the Nat- ural Spread-=so there is danger of breaks and misun- derstandings from those they most desire to hold. Approv- al is wanted, but their un- willingness to yield ground mentally often brings sharp eriticism upon them. They should strive for more ad- justability and be less fix- ed in their opinions and the stands they take.

this birthdate, but rather a willingness to work hard --often too hard~- to at- tain the desired place. Am- bition is strong, often so dominating that it obscures more worthy objectives. The attraction is usually to a business career--merchan- dizing or building, contract- ing or land transactions. Whatever they undertake must pay--even if the talent and choice is directed to writ- ing or (with a strong 5th House) to the theatre. We must realize that the ruler- ship here is Saturn and that the Saturn Card is the 10 S-- so success is practically assured, To be satisfied, these natives much deal with peop- le--either by direct con- tact or through work that has a public appeal. Aquar- ians are always free lances and from a wide choice se- lect the occupation that most appeals to themselves-~as long as it promises good re- turns (8 D-- Uranus Card). Writers, actors, clergymen, statesmen or counselors and group leaders can succeed. Ruler- Neptune. Here is a strong sense of service and an urge to make known the ideals to which they are dedicated. There is usually great attraction for the sea and many are suc- cessful in commerce, naval

affairs and foreign trade. Good diagnosticians, dealers in cosmetics and perfumes or oil operators are found here-- also writers and philosophers. but often beset with obstacles and disappointments owing to their tendency to choose the easiest way. Subject to many changes caused by circumstan- ces. Make good writers, lectu- rers, journalists--sometimes actors or artists. Are able as trial lawyers. Not sufficient- ly patient or far-seeing to be- come counselors or trustees. Ruler- Venus. Are public spirited and phil- anthropic. Want peace and secu- rity and strong home roots, with luxurious surroundings. Are successful in land deals, horticulture and anything that deals with natural products and resources. Love of nature is strong and they should live in the country a part of the year. Many poets and authors are found in this date. If at- tracted to public life, they make good politicians and gov ermment officials. In business, can succeed as head of large manufacturing concerns. Restless and active; always have more than one iron in

the fire. Both social and bus- iness contacts are essential; must always have an audience. Easy flow of words and make good journalists, columnists and correspondents. Many are attracted to science (mainly from curiosity) and the church (mainly from their sense of drama). They move quickly and change frequently--in the search for improvement. Displaces the A H in the Nat. Sp.

In the Mundane Spread we have found that the 3 H is the "first step out", Since the Mundane Spread is a picture of our departure from God's Plan, we may regard the 3 H as the original parent of all the mistakes--"departures"--that follow, the Adam that symbolizes the fall of man. We displaced the desire for Love (A H) as the ru- ler of our lives with a mental uncertainty about love in general and love of what or whom in par- ticular. Perhaps it is the sub- conscious realization of this great error that so often makes the love life of the 3 H an un- satisfying-- or tragic-- thing, and causes the eternal quest for the perfect love-- and the doubt of it when found. As far as the emotions are con- cerned, life for the 3 H is a se- ries of trials and errors. Its True Place (Natural Spread) is the Mars Card in the Mercury Line, so there is no lack of activity in the quest. Their Fellow men never give them a crown of fidelity but it is not so much a question of fickleness or promiscuity in their case as an inner need for the one love that satisfies the Self (A H). The pity of it is that the criti- eal faculty (Mercury) steps in and sows the seeds of questioning and doubt.

Socially, they are usually charming and attractive people-- welcome guests and perfect hosts; it is only in the closer relation- ships that the strain becomes a break. They are always intelligent and informed (Mercury Card in the Mercury Line), but since their un- certainty extends to their mental processes they do not always dis- tinguish clearly between right and wrong, truth and falsehood-- unless the early training has fixed the distinction for them. They are of- ten dominated in youth by the mo- ther, later by the wife and there- fore have the best chance for self- development away from home. They should never marry anyone who in- terferes with their freedom-- even in its uncertainty or misuse. There are but two birthdates ruled by this card, therefore but two types of people to consider- the Sagittarian (November 30) and the Capricornian (December 28). lines of thought. Should be very particular to give cred- it when the idea is not orig- inal. Usually very talkative-- even verbose. Good story tell- ers and good critics; never

at a loss for a word. Should guard against prying into pri- vate affairs-- and gossip. Attracted to intelligent wom- en but are not happy if mar- ried to one of intellect super- ior to their own. Married women are apt to be interfered with by their mothers-in-law or sis- ters-in-law. Often form a valu- able friendship with a wom- an of spiritual insight and intuitional gifts. Through her they have an opportunity to develop their own. Enjoy social activities and like to entertain groups of people who are important in the pro- fessional and literary world. Success is indicated in any line of activity seriously undertaken, Work well with friends, especially men. Are depressed by an atmosphere of inharmony, which interferes with success. May become im- portant in the business or professional world. Can suc- cessfully combine business and social activities. Put over deals at dinner parties; transact business at the club, etc. The women may become soc- ial lights, leaders of salons, etc.

Apt to change the vocation of first choice. If finances are limited or doubtful, they keep changing occupations in the hope of greater security. Will always walk out if there is stagnation or monotony-- but they usually walk into uncertainty in that event. There is often some burden connected with home or the emotional life that inter- feres with accumulating an income that is satisfactory. Uncertainty may extend to health-- of themselves or others-- and interfere with business and finances. Worry over money is apt to cause illness; distrust of the phy- sician interferes with recov- ery. Health insurance is a Must for these people; other- wise their surroundings in illness may be very uncomfor- table and the care they re- ceive insufficient or unsat- isfactory.

There is always a desire for occult knowledge, but there is likewise doubt and skepti- cism about accepting it as truth. It is only those who are willing to make sacrifi- ces for it who are able to profit by it. May have psy- chic gifts but hesitate to use them. If not adherents to strict honesty, can lead “double lives" successfully. Unless problems are over-~ come in the earlier years, the end of life is not likely to be happy or satisfactory. Security must be built up be- fore 50. They should have one person in the life who will remain faithful and devoted. Travel proves disappointing and does not help the rest- lessness of the later years. Their own devotion to an ob- ject, a principle or an in- dividual would go far toward overcoming the difficulty. Good sportsmanship and pop- ularity belong to this birth- date. Clever with words-- but seldom show their hand. Have great influence with crowds; understand mass psychology. Good orators, writers, states- men; often have inventive gifts. Ruler- Saturn.

Have fits of stubborness but are actually concerned about their future. Fear insecurity. Should avoid medicine as a ca- reer; dislike physical con- tacts. Interested in large en- terprises; ambitious. May be- come political leaders, states- men. More faithful than Nov. 30. Clubs - Mental Attitudes 3C¢ - Uncertainty in beliefs. 3.in C- Ideas from Kindred Minds. The Mars Card in the Saturn Line. Displaces the 5 D in the Nat. Sp. You will note that this card carries a heavy burden of 3-- with a strong emphasis on "heavy" since it falls in the Saturn Line under the emphatic influence of Mars. Two 3's fall together-- the card itself and its Mercury Card; its True Place is in the Mars Line; so a "Mars-Saturn Conjunction" defines the undercurrent through- out the life. Perhaps a slight saving grace lies in the fact that the True Place is in the Venus position--but doubt about the choice or stability of love- relationships is likely to be as disturbing as the shifting mental attitudes. The cry for Peace seems never to be answered for these people until they learn to depend solely upon themselves and their inner resources.

The displacement of the 5 D bespeaks money as a major factor in uncertainty. The periods of steady and dependable income are few and far between. There is often a change-- or several chan- ges- in occupation in the hope of finding means of increasing the income. Despite the shifts in view- point, there is seldom a desire to change themselves- their own established concepts and intoler- ance of the new and untried; it is partly the inability to decide on the right course, partly stub- bornness. All Clubs feel that their mental attitudes are right. They do have an inner knowledge-- but they don't always use it. Usually willing to acknowledge their mistakes and willing to try again-~- but with no confidence in the new effort; mentally, they are beaten before they start. The more developed ones-- those who are aware of their intuitive gifts and of their inheritance of knowledge-- will invariably turn to metaphysics for their solution-- and will find it. Their security then becomes dependable and they abandon the role of "Doubting Thom- ases",

There may be some misfortune connected with death and inherit- ance; something way interfere with receiving their rights, payment of debts owned them or money that is legally their own. There is also some danger of accident in these birthdates-- Mars-Saturn being a combination to watch, and uncer- tainty of direction being a menance at all times. Decisions should be made care- fully and slowly-- then believed in and held to. They should be will- ing to abandon tradition in this changing world and to accept new values in the name of growth. They should strive, through meditation and the cultivation of their own spiritual power, to work with what the soul knows from past experience, not against it. Planetary Sequence for the 3 C 38 A sense of insecurity may last until the 28th year. Always two patterns to pur- sue, two paths to choose from. May have two occupa- tions at the same time. Many short trips. Any business will require money and ef- fort.

Constantly on the go. Have at least two irons in the fire at a time. Should culti- vate a hobby or sideline that holds their interest. Are ad- aptable and versatile, but not good stickers unless interest- ed in a cause or an individual. Well suited to public life-- stage, politics, lecturing. Clever and good investigators-- but not researchers. Good at salesmanship if allowed to travel; should never be tied to a desk or a boring occupa- tion. Skilful with their hands. Ruler- The Moon.

Can deal successfully with children. The women make good nurses, devoted and motherly. Home is their center. Will take many responsibilities of others on their own shoulders. Willing to sacrifice for loved ones. Like to be before the public or be considered the center of some branch of public life. Have a strong sense of drama and appre- ciation of music. Can succeed at teaching, direction, dressmaking, millinery. Have more power and strength than the other 3 C-- and are aware of it. A law unto them- selves, not usually orthodox. Dictatorial in own line of work. Best of all professions for them is the theatre-- but not ne- cessarily acting. Make good di- rectors and producers. May at- tain a high place if willing to work hard and stick to their de- cisions. Work may be interfered with through ill health. Good executors, statesmen, leaders. Fundamentally spiritual, but not always considered so. Can commercialize art. Aug.23. Ruler- Sun or Mercury. Leo-Vir.Want power but prefer to

leave responsibility to others. If in the army will al- ways "'pass the buck''. If Leo predominates, may be perform- ers-- actors, musicians, etc. If Virgo, good scientists-- es- pecially chemists. Can be very calculating. Can succeed in banking, real estate or as pro- fessors and teachers of adults. Investigating turn of mind; of- ten have wanderlust-- from men- tal curiosity. Subject to ner- vous indigestion and should be careful of diet. Good in the publishing field, preferring educational books. Many are successful writers, Sensitive and nervous. Dis- satisfied with own accomp- lishment. Need praise and ap- proval. Have keen minds and high ideals. Should guard a- gainst depression and discour- agement. Long for success and try many ways of attaining it-- not fully trusting any of them. Inclined to belittle their own abilities. Great readers and investigators. The most spir- itually-minded of the 3 C. Choice and discriminating in taste. Writers, publishers, in- vestigators. More concerned with the interests of others than their own. Often talent- ed in art but cannot commer- cialize it. Lack self-assurance Ruler- Venus.

Independent; inclined to self-indulgence. Should guard against selfishness. Origin- al in thought. Life often dis- turbed by outside interference. Can commercialize art. Success- ful at law, music, plastics. Writers,-- sometimes poets. There is a keen desire for Truth, but it is not always sought beyond the limits of expediency. The less develop- ed ones become crafty and se- cretive, unwilling to display their hand and lose some fan- cied advantage. Often feel that the end justifies the means. Those who are aware of their destiny become great ex- ponents and teachers of meta- physics, Among them there is full realization that they are but agents and trustees of whatever gifts they have and that their minds must be kept unceasingly on the high- er levels in order to receive the direction and inspiration that will not fail them. They are eager to give forth their knowledge for the benefit of others.

May become victims of unre- liable friends or associates. If they have beauty, material security or unusual advantages are subject to jealousy and gossip. Apt to make a marriage that is disappointing. Should be wary of giving confidence or releasing own rights; may have trouble in recovering them. Because of affectional disappointment are often driv- en back upon themselves that they may re-discover their own power and self-sufficiency. Partnerships or other close associations with men not usually successful. In the case of women, if there is compatibility, the happiness may not last. Husband, son or brother may be taken by death or a separation may be forced. If not compatible, men may cause trouble in finances, unreasonable demands or dom- ination. Changes in occupation-~ ei- ther forced or voluntary. If they adhere to the same type of business, will change the method or approach, Business often benefitted by travel. Should seek business associ- ates among powerful and in- fluential friends. Harmony and friendliness essential to business success. Restless. Should not remain too long in one place.

Note that this is one of the fixed cards-- always found in the same position and always the same as the card it dis- places. Here being the J H under Saturn, the element of sacrifice is emphasized-- u- sually because of some forced limitation or through the ill- ness of some one toward whom there is an obligation--real or fancied. May become a mar- tyr to duty. These people have healing power and can ac- complish great good through it. which discourages and blocks them. Should analyze causes and refuse to be influenced by others. Not well adapted to dealings with labor groups or likely to have loyalty from employees. Unless highly de- veloped and sure of their ground, should avoid psychism. Contact with the spirit world is apt to be disappointing and unreliable. If spiritually en- lightened, can receive direct illumination and sure guidance. Mundanely, 9 is disappoint- ment and loss; spiritually it is fulfilment. If the mental attitudes (A S) have been rightly directed, peace will come in the later years. Mat- ters at a distance are not favored; travel (after 50) is disappointing. Should not live near the sea.

Can. Fear insecurity. Very de- pendent upon others; want comfort and cannot always earn enough to assure it. Do not take kindly to interfer- ence, but will sacrifice for loved ones. Demand a home but dislike any limitation in it. Dominated by a desire for love but are often disappointed. Given more to philosophy and theories derived from tradi- tion and past experience than to progress and changes. Are useful in a public capacity-- as politicians, welfare work- ers, reformers (according to orthodox rules). Like a posi- tion of prominence but need support from others, Leo Are natural leaders and pro- moters but have a sense of is- olation when they become prom- inent. Love deeply and are loy- al; when the idol is broken, have no desire to rebuild. Too sensitive for their own good. Public-spirited; able and suc- cessful in public life. Self- protective because uncertain of the future. Succeed best as a head of own business. Are dynamic writers, good propa- gandists. Can succeed in mer- chandizing and manufacturing. Are dramatic and effective.

Vir. More material-minded and less aware of their true des- tiny than many other 3 D. Apt to be swamped with detail and have a narrow viewpoint. Can become penurious if they are not watchful. Analytical and meticulous. Neglect no small points but often lose the lar- ger vision, Can succeed as public servants, writers or lawyers (corporation, not tri- al). Good researchers. Want practical results and good re- turn for their efforts. Want peace and easy con- ditions, but uncertainty in- terferes with contentment. More protected than many 3 D but always fear the poorhouse. Are intuitive and find many "answers" -- then doubt them. Attracted to the limelight and can become good actors, playwrights or lecturers. Good hosts and entertainers. Love the country and even go in for sports to keep them in the open-- although bet- ter adapted for an artistic or literary career. Very va- cillating in opinions and in- capable of firm decisions. Ruler- Mars,

and indecision but give the impression of being very set and sure of themselves. Suf- fer through love but remain loyal and continue to sacri- fice. Keep their troubles to themselves. Have a keen under- standing of human foibles and an over-ready philosophy to apply to them, Often attract- ed to drugs or medicine and ean succeed in all branches of healing. Keen searchers for truth-- and causes. Out- wardly critical. Good at man- ual arts-- sculpture, model- ing in clay, etc. Want success and will make frequent changes to obtain it. Need congenial ‘surroundings and harmony in associations. Sensitive and magnetic-- of- ten very aware spiritually. Must never deviate from their ideals or will end in failure. Inwardly know the Law, but fear it-- and fear not to fol- low it. Meet many disappoint- ments and frustrations. Often put in a position that is for- eign to their nature or beyond their ability. Good writers.

skeptical about all friend- Uran. ships. May meet opposition 9D in efforts to raise money. Are swayed by gossip or may become victims of it if the life is not above reproach. Business competitors bring changes and shifts of poji- cy, causing fluctuation in income and failure to become established. May be interest- ed in distant transactions, acting as brokers or agents. Often cause their own finan- cial burdens by refusing to follow advice or becoming set in their own opinions. Apt to act on impulse and then make changes to cover up the mistake. If associated with a hard- working woman can be con~ siderably helped. Should see that employees are reliable. If a physician, should seek help from a woman nurse or doctor. In any line, a woman manager is advisable; she will help to mitigate any loss. Best to hire women ser- vants or business associates whenever possible. Will often make money through them.

Note that there are 2 Jacks here; the card that is dis- placed is the J D. Positive- ly, Jacks are valuable and important; negatively, they are sly, crafty, frivolous and irresponsible. Under the disciplinary Saturn, this is a warning to live strictly and soberly-~ no drinking or later hours; no self-indul- gence that interferes with work or duty. Obligations may be increased by some mem- ber of the family-- father, brother or son who is a prob- lem, an expense, is afflict- ed or in poor health. This card in this position should be regarded as a challenge. If investing in real estate should avoid anything that is speculative in nature or that promises a quick turn- over in a re-sale, Invest- ments of any kind should be for a long term. Should keep out of any difficulty that involves labor groups or trade unions. Should never be involved in their dis- putes or try to uphold their rights. May have trouble with employees. If employed, may not be adequately paid. Travel will not be satis~ factory or reminerative. Should not live near the water; if there is a tenden- cy to colds, rheumatism, etc. it will be aggravated. Best to avoid any occupation con- nected with shipping, li- quids or medicines; perfume or oil.

0} BATT *SasTZoToyoAsd ‘si1eydo -soltyd ‘s10zei0 ‘siejt1M *pa -ssoidep ueqjo ‘snotzes *4yqyTM S@ATT 03 prey puke OTqQeITIIT AzeA suooaq Aew Axiom y8noryy *s2z9y30 Jo uotutdo ayy uodn quepuedsp aq jou pue AqTINDes FSUUT ateyz e2eATIZI[NS oF pseu Aoul ‘Ivey ST AreMod puy eoetd UMO Itsy} UO sdUezSTSUT ITeYI JO stseq ey] "wey YIM seTez -193UT oy suokue JTpazostp 03 sy z3ueT suos 02 08 Aum pue -- AQTAoyjNe Iyey] suotzsenb Ao wey} seuTysqno oym ouodue eje1aTOR Jou {Tt Aeyy ‘sdrys -UoOT}BTeT [eUOsSiad out das10 uajjo sseutzjed pue sseumozieu ‘ssoujeer3 yo squowetTa ejtdseq *aoUeA[OIUT ST Z[Ney Burpueqs -Jno ey *eAetiTeq Aayq yoTym ut asned Aue 103 saATasueyy Sutieds ynoyaTA yom [TM pue suotjtsod jusuuzeA03 07 payins T1284 ere Aayy, *squeares 9T{qGnd Sutpuejsgno pue sjotajed [e -Aoy ‘szeyuTYy] usey Aueu peonp -oid sey e7ep sty] *snoT] -Tque ‘pertdsut ‘snoarey solid square, the cube. 4 is our Fenced Field, our Security-- and therefore our Protection. "Sentinels" are at the 4 cor- ners of the field, assuring our safety within it; the field it- self is ours-- to build in.

4 is the symbol for Earth, the 4th "element". We "fell" into "matter" that we might work our way out of it, so we know that despite the security and protec- tion that surrounds us, there is to be no idleness within the God- given enclosure that is 4. We are to build a firm and unassailable foundation (4); we are to labor (4) with concentration (4) and diligence (4) and secure (4) for ourselves the right to work out our pattern in freedom from un- conquerable obstacles, poverty, heartache and failure. We must bear in mind that 4 is the num- ber of Saturn, and that he hands out the rewards if and when they are earned-- and only so. As Birth Cards, 4's are among the most fortunate. They give us a wonderful start-- with protect- ion, security, sufficiency-- but like every other card and every birth pattern, the course of our lives and their finish is up to us. Of the 4 spots or "sentinels" two are matter and two are spirit; in 4 they are united. He who at- tempts to separate them builds his foundation on sand and the wind and floods sweep it from under him. He finds himself in an empty field-- save for Saturn.

The 4th Sphere on the Tree of Life is Chesed-- Mercy-- the realm of Jupiter. The 4th Statement in the Pattern on the Trestleboard is "From the Limitless Substance I draw all things needful, both spiritual and material". To the Pythagoreans 4 was the Sacred Number, for its elements-- 1, 2, 3 and 4 when summed up are equal to 10 "which is so perfect that we can go no further, but to increase we must return to the Monad", (Wynn Westcott). For our 4's, then, the prime admonition is to BUILD. HEARTS are to build their secu- rity on love for their fellow man if they may hope for love in their own lives. CLUBS are to build on Knowledge- to be shared and dissemin- ated for the profit, edu- cation and growth of all. DIAMONDS are to build on VALUES that are everlasting. SPADES are to labor, with hands and hearts and minds for the WISDOM that is of God, That is their foundation and security. Their miss- ion is to shed the Light they have found on every dark corner, on all blind eyes and every burdened heart.

As in the Tarot, the 4 is an Emperor. 4's have come to be and can be in command of every situation that arises. Their task is organization. Are interested in quick turnovers and speedy returns from any investment. The ability to ac- cumulate money through their own efforts gives them great satisfaction. There is usual- ly a good financial background to work from and rarely any actual lack in their early en- vironment. They are inclined to be practical and level- headed when it comes to expen- ditures for education or any necessary equipment. Sat. Strong home ties and depend- 6S ence on cooperation. They dislike changes of home and

in family relationships. Hos- pitable and socially inclined. They like to surround them- selves with congenial groups and prefer their own homes as a meeting place. In intimate associations they are attract- ed to people of good standing but will not exclude those of limited means if they have oth- er assets that meet their stan- dards. Like to join art, edu- cational or cultural classes. Fond of entertainment~- usu- ally high class. Good enter- tainers themselves and cordial hosts. Are inclined to spend Uran. considerable time in dancing, QH theatre-going and general ex- citement -- and later have a sense of regret over the waste of effort and the lack of ful- filment derived. They want amusement and diversion but do not want to sacrifice any of their power through the ill affects of self-indulgence. Good sports and willing to pay for their fun-- but are not pleased with themselves when anything is neglected.

Benefit by following their intuitions-- which are re~ liable, There is a definite cleavage between the positive and negative aspects of this position. For the positives, harmony and security are as- sured by diligently working for it-- "keeping the wheels greased". The negatives are likely to fall into a rut be- cause of laziness and self- satisfaction. If sufficiently provided for they find it eas- ier to rest on their oars and be content with less than to bestir themselves and achieve a better situation. These people have a founda~ tion of good health but may injure it by self-indulgence or by involving themselves with personalities that are undesirable or unsatisfactory. The consequence is worry that disturbs the health and may set up a chronic condition that is hard to overcome. The conflict usually comes from operating under the Person- ality Card rather than the Birth Card-- especially in the case of women. 4 H has an innate knowledge of Divine Law and should not deviate from it. Are attracted to the drugless field and are natur- al healers.

When used constructively, intuition and inspiration are strong in this card. If problems are to be met or im- portant decisions to be made, serious reflection and medi- tation will produce a safe guide, Mistakes in invest- ments-- especially real est- ate-- are rarely made; deal- ings with groups and organi- zations are profitable and successful; employees are co- operative and faithful. This is one of the Patriot cards inasmuch as there is no departure from fundamentals, They will fight for their interpretation of the law if they believe it is a good law to start with.

Between the men and women of this card there is a basic equal- ity. The Queen of Clubs is as pow- erful in her way as either the King or the Jack; her judgment is usually as sound and her intui- tion superior. Then, too, she oc~ cupies the central position in the Natural Spread and in this age of intuition and extra-sen- sory perception, she is given her place of equality. In the realm of politics and political econo- my she is, in many instances, u- surping the place formerly held exclusively by men and it is her job, as the 4 C, to prove that she can hold it-- and perhaps im- prove upon it. The Jupiter posi- tion bespeaks his blessing on this situation because it is evolution- ary. The keynote for this man- woman combination is Cooperation. Together they will gain more wis- dom than would be possible alone. The keen intelligence of this Mars-Jupiter conjunction makes these people intolerant of stupid- ity and triviality for they are fundamentally mental (Clubs) and they realize that growth and ex- pansion (Jupiter) are possible on- ly through constant activity (Mars) along mental lines (C). The undeveloped ones are apt to talk much and say little. Their

right to the 4 C as a Birth Card gives them a hearing, but unless they have learned to clarify and organize their ideas they will not hold their audience. These are the arguers and de- baters-- the lawyers with volum- inous briefs, the speakers with lengthy paragraphs, the writers whose introductions are longer than the subject matter, Unless they are lazy and careless-- which is rare-- they are concerned on- ly with matters of importance. they are seldom willing to waste their time or brains on inconse- quentials. They are good travelers and interesting companions-- always ready to point out something that everybody else has missed. They are, of necessity, usually at- tracted to professions and ment- al pursuits-- teaching, lectur- ing, philosophy, education, pol- itics and economics, If in busi- ness, they want their indepen- dence and an opportunity to pro- ject their own ideas. Only in rare cases are the women domes- tically inclined; their best ex- pression is in an independent business or in the literary or educational field.

2D tance, for it represents an avenue of freedom. While still young they learn to bar- gain. Business propositions come suddenly and unexpected- ly; travel and changes without warning. Must have occupation that moves fast. Should av- oid medicine as a career ex- cept as mental or nerve spe- cialists. Good talkers, lec- turers. Interested in law, diplomacy or the market, Can succeed as engineers or sci- entists, Should guard against superficiality, procrastina- tion. Apt to scatter the for- ces. Work best with others. Ruler- The Moon Self-indulgent; usually put on much weight. Must take good care of teeth; may tend to crumble. Natural a- bility for dealing with oth- ers. Often talented-- in singing or working in metal or clay. Successful in work with the ground-- raising commodities, growing flow- ers, profiting by oil. May become food experts, diet- icians, prize chefs. May have talent for the theatre. Ruler- The Sun.

Responsible leaders. Want public life or prominence. Dramatic, spectacular. Act- ors, dramatists. Want to dis- cover or initiate something of importance. May become public philanthropists, wel- fare promoters, art sponsors. Charitable-- but want their gifts publicized. Rulers-- The Sun or Merc. Less spectacular than July 24, often better bal- anced, but equally gifted. May become outstanding in dramatic or musical field. May devote their lives to a cause; have strong opinions and beliefs. Sensitive, but practical, Love the country, children and dogs. Idealists but remain practical, Excel- lent parents. Have intuition and inspi- ration-- but fight them; want to remain practical. Often choose the easiest way through laziness or expedi- ency. If remain true to the best of themselves, can ac~ complish much. May be drawn to law, counseling, psych- ology, research-- especially of disease. Want personal freedom. Should choose an occupation that is mental. If tied to home, should have a hobby. Friendly, popular. Busi- ness interests advanced

by personality. Can become a power in groups. Like to shine -- as socialites, en- tertainers, artists. Usually well groomed. Women succeed as stylists, models, beauti- cians. Work best if married or in partnership. May meet opposition from family. Can succeed as lawyers or poli- ticians. Want to promote what they believe in. Will espouse cau- ses; public spirited. Intense and dramatic; usually have mu- sical talent. Not always suc- cessful in marriage; have i- deals and want others to live up to them. Good government officials, statesmen. Have a long-range vision. Men (espe- cially) should guard against self-indulgence. ten bound to a routine oc- cupation. A karmic date; have come to settle obligations. Honest, but reckless; care- less of consequences. Nervous and tense. Can write, lecture, sell- but only if interested. Associations necessary}; there is an early fear of be- ing left with responsibility and decisions. Much involved with agreements, messages, communications, letters and telephone calls. May engage in work associated with these things. Always want things definite and clarified-- set down on paper. Except for the birthdates under the mental signs, are not especially in- terested in education.

May form a platonic friend- ship that lasts a long time. Are not eager for the respon- sibility of marriage-- but may marry for protection and security. May make many friends in some group activity-- often religious, Marriage not always satisfactory; some unforeseen obstacle often arises that cau- ses disappointment, but if a break occurs, it is usually brought about by the other partner. Want peace and harmonious conditions. Eager for suf- ficient knowledge to bring a- bout improvement. Feel that it can be found in experience rather than in books. If con- tacts are congenial and condi- tions fairly smooth, may get into a rut and stay there. Will have progress if they make an effort. If in a commercial occupa- tion they are likely to remain there. If spiritually developed, can become mental healers. Can have good, steady income if are diligent and con- centrated in work and do not seek unreasonable expansion. If meney dominates, their nat- ural protection is lost and

business difficulties arise. Subject to disappointment in work-associates-- usually from failure to assume their own share of responsibility. The 6 S is one of the "ini- tiate"” cards. The enlighten- ed 4 D native will apply met- aphysics to his business. The mother, wife or some female relative may pres-~ ent a problem or become a means of expense. There is indicated here a karmic at- tachment-- possibly a debt-- to be discharged. For these people themselves, health may be a problem, but they are usually well taken care of. There may be marriage to an older woman (in the case of the men) and she may prove to be a hindrance. If a woman, marriage is desired but is not always a means to happiness. Find contentment in deal- ing in real estate, prod- ucts of the earth, building, etc. Should ally themselves with progressive and humani- tarian groups, fraternities, etc, Can become outstanding leaders. Such a powerful card under Uranus indicates a val- uable intuitional gift. If this is cultivated, can de- pend upon guidance. Unless wholly self-centered, will be divinely directed to chan- nels of success,

to be more financially pro- tected than the earlier. May have a legacy or substantial inheritance-- although prob- ably not without some confu- sion attached to it, These people should have no desire to retire from business until far advanced in years. Will be happier and more useful if they remain active. When they forget themselves, become success- ful in business, useful in the community. Concerned with homes, families, housing, etc. Can deal in commodities-- what the public needs and wants, Paternal (or maternal) and protective. Like to shine as public benefactors. Nervous and restless; never feel they are fully appreciated. Have a sense of service but are often too lazy to let it man- ifest. Want comfort with-

Very keen for success. Do well with business if al- ready established-- but want it on a large scale. Always want value received. Are ei- ther self-centered or just the opposite-- humanitarian and self-sacrificing. May be attracted to religious groups; if the self-seeking type, can make the connection pay. Are good organizers. Can succeed as actors but an equal num- ber do just as well as mer- chants and manufacturers. Inclined to science and research. Should follow a line that requires good judg- ment and a keen mind. May be fussy and meticulous if not careful-- also petty in per- sonal relationships. Sharp and clever-- sometimes too much so, If religiously in- clined, want demonstrations. CAN be very high-minded. Good writers, teachers, edu- cators; often have keen sense of humor. Shculd avoid work- ing for expediency rather than mutual benefit. Performers, artists, wri- ters. Much attracted to

to be a power in their own circle, Make good lawyers, politicians, leaders. Good agents and go-betweens, Are friendly and co-operative. Have strong metaphysical in- clinations. Emotional and self-indulgent; if overcome those traits, can develop much soul-force. Always have an eye for beauty; always present a good appearance. Need many contacts and chan- ges to keep interest alive. Ruler- Mars. this birthdate, but many prefer the easiest way and will not make the necessary effort, Should avoid getting into a rut. Can gain much hidden knowledge if they seek for it. Fixed and set in their thinking; hard to convince. Have inner fear of lack of money, but do little to fore- stall it. Can succeed in "big" business-- especially manu- facturing. Have a gift for writing, but must cultivate it. Many of the deeper think- ers become poets and compos-~ ers.

Some woman may be very in- fluential in this life. Dependent upon close associa- tions and will take responsi- bility for them. Nervous a- bout making changes, so apt to remain in one place for a long time. Want fréedom and independence in finances and willing to work hard to keep it. Good for travel business, foreign connections, law or diplomacy. Good foreign cor- respondents. Can become able and popular leaders. Usually brought up

in a family that is kindly and affectionate, Dependent upon congenial and understanding teachers for best progress in schools Like to associate with those of kindred minds and in- terests. Cultivation of con- tacts a big factor in future success. This card is better for a man than a woman in the early years; the women are apt to become too dependent emo- tionally, Naturally gravitate toward those who have money or good background. May give too much importance to prominence and social standing. Are attracted to art and want to accomplish something in an artistic line-- but only if it can be made to pay. If not, it is soon aban- doned. Usually ambitious to make a good marriage--as much for prestige as for security. Want beauty in their surround- ings. Should strive to look beyond appearances for values. The original displacement of the 10 C indicates suc- cess in any line diligently pursued; the 8 S under Mars confirms it. They prefer work in organizations where men pre- dominate and want any business they are connected with to be capable of expansion. The men

There is a strong desire for money-- for entertaining and display, but also for charity. These people are natural phil- anthropists and make large con- tributions to causes in which they believe. They want every- body to be comfortable and at peace; they believe that sup- ply is adequate for the entire world, if properly distrib- uted. Should cultivate the habit of "not letting the left hand know what the right hand gives". Are too apt to want their names attached to their gifts. May fail to reach their ob- jectives through such a strong desire for money that worry interferes with their accomplishment. They know bet- ter, but there is always a fear of lack. Should cultivate a keener sense of values and realize that work and self- discipline will bring their own rewards. Can succeed (and profit) in work along healing lines, Make good consultants and diagnosticians. Can al- ways overcome their difficul- ties and are always protected. Some woman of strong per- sonality or psychic power

may have a marked influence on their lives. In business, should have a good business woman for keeping accounts and directing expenditures, Such a one is es- pecially valuable in any work along unusual or original lines, Should not adhere too closely to traditional methods, Can suc- ceed-- often brilliantly-- in untried fields, especially if the results would be of univer- sal benefit. Any inventive a- bility should be cultivated. Especially in later years, money will be spent for trav- el- usually with satisfactory results. The changes that come are apt to be beneficial and agreeable, After 45 the occupa- tion itself may have radical changes. These should not be resisted as they usually work out to advantage. ety, Opportunity, Travel, Es- cape and fluctuating Life Experi- ence. tion of the spots and you will see that 5 is a Cross. This is logical enough when we reflect that 5 is the Number of Man and his (original) 5 senses. Life experience is of necess- ity a cross. Changes and surprises we regard with disfavor since it is but human to fear the unfamiliar or unknown. We may regard 5 solely in this light if we choose to-- but the wise ones re- cognize Change as Growth, the re- lease from the well-worn rut as Opportunity. Whatever our point of view, the 5 natives are restless--and usually dissatisfied. They want change because they do not want what they have, but as soon as the change is made--still fear- ful of what lies ahead--they want to change the change. There is always the chance that more freedom lies ahead--and Freedom is their battle-cry. It is difficult for these peo- ple to concentrate. They stand in the center and strive to reach all corners at the same time. They are much like the mythical knight of old who mounted his horse and rode off in all directions. Always seek- ing new places and new faces, new occupations and new loves, new ex- periences and new changes.

This is by no means the whole story of 5. While the choice is for experience rather than achieve- ment, 5 gains great understanding of his fellow man; he is strict- ly honest and strictly just (un- less operating wholly negatively) for his 6th sense tells him that only thus may he expect honesty and justice from others. He un- derstands the 5 steps of progres- sion in the scheme of creation-- stone, plant, animal, man, God-- and he feels, if he seeks far e- nough and keeps moving and obser- ving, he will reach the topmost. The discontent is not always "divine"; it easily descends to chronic disaffection with people, surroundings and life in general, Abhorring routine, disclaiming responsibility, fearful of any inroad on their personal freedom, they are not always dependable or faithful; their sense of jus- tice does not extend to ties that could-= and perhaps should-- bind them. Their own growth is vital but they should realize that it is not a very laudable type of growth when it is achiev- ed at the expense of those toward whom they have obligations. HEARTS have the problem of unsat- isfied emotions. CLUBS of a chronic mental rest- lessness-- and no peace.

5's mist realize the differ- ence between good and bad rest- lessness. Changes imposed from without, in accordance with evo- lutionary law carry us up the spi- ral. Voluntary changes from a sense of discontent deprive us of our right to final peace. ‘op AsyQ usym atdoad ~nzaapuom aze Asya !AaT[TqTsuodsaz ,TRuOTy -ome a3BATIIND prnoys HC *asuad -Woda1 02 JYZTA 9yQ peurwa aaey pue TBAOT pue ngtanp ‘tnzdqey aq 03 Q103ya Ue apew aaBy AdyQ asneo -aq ATTeNSN st JT asBo sty UT ynq ~-90UR}TIaYyUT pue szzTS Aq qtyord oy sauo ,,AyonT,, OSTe a1B ardyT *way2 JO 3saq ayz axyeM pue sad -uejJsundato atayQ ydaoDe oym sauo @STM ‘asanoo jo ‘aie arayy

They are always seeking oppor- tunities to make money for themselves-- mainly because dependence upon others keeps them tied to home and family. There may be many occasions for travel, but the money prob- lem interferes with their en- joyment. and fluctuation. These are Sat. the people who are given to 5D

summer attachments and vaca- tion crushes-- but a change of scene usually brings a change of mind and they decide that the new love is not the right one. If actually committed, many will go through with the bargain but will soon begin to look around for means of es- cape. It is difficult for them to form roots; when they do they regard them as chains or ruts and are often made ill by a sense of monotony. Dealings with men are sel- dom satisfactory-- for ei- Qs ther sex. These natives are and an eagerness to make new contacts, dealings with them of laziness in many and they will not take the trouble to work out their problems. If they do they invariably have the reward of overcoming and can proceed to success. All business dealing must be carried out in strict honesty. It is easy for these people to exaggerate or distort. They are often saddled with partners or as sociates who fail to carry their share of the load; sub- ject to trickery and deceit on the part of others and must be careful to have all transactions fully under- stood and recorded. Are not well favored in legal mat- ters and in case of dispute should always try to settle out of court.

Health may be a problem unless well fortified in youth. Money is spent for care and remedies that never seem satisfactory or the health of some member of the family may interfere with business and be costly. This is a situation where change is advisable for health is often benefitted by getting away from home. Apt to make many changes in doctors and nurses. Often increase their ailments by trying too many remedies and "cures". or fraternities, want to run things and introduce va- rious systems and unusual i- deas. Are often benefitted by association with a hard- working, practical woman who is capable of assuming res- ponsibility. Such an associa- tion may turn out to be a life saver financially. The Saturn Card in the Mercury L Displaces the 5 H in the Nat. Sp.

There is a close tie-up with the 5 H (by Displacement) and we may expect to find many similar problems, Since the 5 C is less dependent upon affection than the 5 H, the difficulties will be less of the heart than of the mind. Nevertheless, there is emotional imbalance and a sense of insecu- rity in relationships. By rulership, although Saturn is most strongly emphasized in the mental reactions, the True Place offers a measure of protection by its Jupiter position in the Mars Line. In the 5 H this is lacking. The 5 C is a young soul at cross purposes with itself. Rest- less, inquiring, seeking, these natives are never satisfied with the "answers" they find in life- experience, They are constantly meeting with obstacles and never know quite what to do with them. They want constant movement and wide travel-- eager to go anywhere rather than remain where they are. The mistake they make is dashing off on impulse, refusing to plan in advance or count the cost. If they would but engrave on their minds the familiar railroad sign of the country crossroads--"Stop, Look and Listen''-- they would come closer to peace of mind.

They have the equipment for acquiring a rounded-out knowledge; they have the natural inclination toward a sound edutation but they usually lack the faculty for apply- ing what they learn, having a conviction that all that is gen- eral and usual and useful does sense of ego is strong; they feel themselves to be special people, especially singled out for trouble. The brilliant exceptions are those who educate themselves in some specialty that interests them and then pursue it to a successful outcome, They honestly try for a foundation and fight against the tangents that would lure them away from the necessity for building. They seek knowledge for the sake of its value and the enlighten- ment it will bring them rather than for satisfaction of idle cu- riosity. If they are willing to learn by experience and profit by information gained, success is as- sured. Not often well suited for mar- riage, the secret wish is there and the experiment usually made. If married, there are apt to be se- cret jealousies and suspicions as well as a change in their own feel- ings.

They never remain in the place of birth; they never stay long in the same school or finish the course they begin; they never adhere to the same line of business-- or if they do, they make such radical changes in it that it is unrecognizable. They must have an outlet for their natural mental keeness and the expression of their opinions. The better the educational back- ground, the more likelihood there is for success, They have no set creed or religion and what they de- vise for themselves is never satis- fying. Peace of mind comes from the willingness to accept life as it must be lived-- constructively. -Nsy *lezyo 07 sey ay Surry zKue qdaooe uayy [ [TM fisyoeaz jen -jtatds [eTuesuo. e 03 payoeq -78 9wodaq ssezunN wsTTenzTI JO [NjJuz09g5 -euawousyd atTyd -4sd yQqM aadmeq jou prnoys *Jooid Sutyaes hj uejJsuOD siy ‘aFT] 22Ie] UT JeTTeq Jo woz aSueys ABW ‘sio,TTes poo’ shem -T® 10N ‘aTdoad pue satzq -uno> u8tei10F ut paqsei1aquT *paqBATITND aq prnoys

ive. Often pioneers in lit- erature or philosophy. Have artistic and musical talent and could make a great suc- cess by cultivating it. Are often careless about keeping agreements. Can train young people and become good leaders if remain impersonal, Ruler- Venus. Artists, sculptors, fi- nanciers and philanthro- pists belong here. May be- come very popular as leaders of groups or projects. Have a greater sense of protect- ion and responsibility than many 5 C, Secretive regard- ing their affections and per- sonal life. Penetrating minds and in- terested in people-~ but im- personally. Hate and fear in~ security. Writers, journal- ists, correspondents.

and eager for changes, but always want roots and have home on their minds while traveling. Ally themselves with groups but remain some- what aloof. Interested in social problems and reli- gion; often engage in insti- tutional work and remain be- hind the scenes. Insistent upon comfort and like te be waited on. Should follow an intellectual pursuit rather than mechanical or commer- cial, although many are bank- ers and merchants. More tra- ditional than other 5 C. Ruler- The Moon or Sun 25, Want recognition and leadership. Not satisfied with small returns for their efforts. Much depressed by temporary failure. Must cul- tivate patience; can then overcome obstacles. Often give up when on the brink of success. Are capable of mak- ing big money if persistent. Brokers, lawyers, merchants, writers. want to be considered an au~ thority in their field. Are creative and original-- also careful and methodical, Apt to be skeptical, but keep on investigating. Easily upset; worry; borrow trouble. The Neptune Card in the Saturn L, Displaces the 9 D in the Nat. Sp.

This Saturn-Neptune "con junc- tion" appears, on first thought, to be an irreconcilable combina- tion, The idealistic, spiritual yearnings of Neptune are apparent- ly brought to a short stop by the relentless materialism of Saturn. That is, indeed, the outcome in the lives of many of these natives. We must bear in mind, however, that Neptune is a gas (or a fog) and that while it does not pene- trate through or break down a mass of rock, it can go under, around, over and above it-- and completely obscure it. The solution is given by the True Place. This, too, is in the Saturn Line but its position is Mars. WORK yourself out of your difficulties; keep your INITIATIVES on the Neptune level; FIGHT for the principles and ideals that are your birthright and Saturn himself will give you the reward for your ef- forts. This does not imply the wis- dom of trying to escape reality;

It simply means overcoming the res~- traints, frustrations and material pressure of Saturn by substituting a determined self-development and spiritual awareness which are in the gift of Neptune. There is the same type of conflict in all cards in this position; with the 5 D we have the uncertainty of financial security to contend with and the constant temptation to yield to expediency. The Cross of this 5 is the burden imposed by decision about values. Not all of these natives are lacking in financial security -- although at some period of the life there will be limitations; the restrictions may come through health or a hampering physical affliction; through uncongenial or troublesome children; through a loved one who is faced with tri- als that cannot be shared or through an environment that is in- harmonious to efforts for self-im- provement. 5 D must choose their course and stick to it. They must seek a location where they can forget themselves and make a contribution, They must abolish fear, worry and uncertainty. They must guard a- gainst a tendency to dominate. They must teach, preach and live the true values they intuitively know.

More than the other 5's, the 5 D has a sense of obligation to- ward family and dependents. They are also less selfish and more considerate. The life is not an easy one but victory is assured through the development of their own soul-power. Money-consciousness is strong. When it is regarded as a means, not an end, it is usually at their disposal when needed. Changes are many; they seldom live near the place of birth. Health problems are diffuclt to diagnose-- and seldom solved by orthodox, medi- cal means, Personalities are im- portant and contacts necessary. Many are attract- ed to the entertainment field, but are not at their best there. If success is gained, their inner longings remain unsatisfied. 22 is the number of the "Master Builder"; any work that tax- es their full capacity must be both practical and ideal- istic. Dislike changes that interfere with their deter- mination to build for per- manence. Their knowledge

is used intuitively-- often "dictated" from higher sour- ces. Are hard workers but dislike routine. Musicians, poets and dramatists may become famous. Usually have some form of bondage to con- tend with-- illness or af- fliction of self or family, financial limitation or a type of service that keeps them overworked. Can suc~ ceed in international af- fairs. Mystical, poetic and mu- sical. Should avoid lazi- ness and getting into a rut. If interested along spirit- ual lines, make good healers and practitioners. Can suc- ceed at nursing, medicine or hotel management. May reach a high place as musicians (especially stringed instru- ments), dancers or singers. The women have strong sense of service unless too self- centered and operating under the QD.

More positive and univer- sally minded than Feb. 20. Have a gift for writing which is not always recog- nized by themselves. The most unselfish of the 5 D; apt to submerge themselves in service. Should develop self-sufficiency and less self-sacrifice. Good trav- elers, artists, philosoph- ers, composers. Good for- eign correspondents, pub- lic servants. If any plan- ets in Fire may become fam- ous as leaders for causes. Should not narrow their field of activity to home interests. Vir. Experts at research. At- tracted to work that deals with health measures and im- provements in living condi- tions. Investigating minds. Interested in mental proces- ces and make good psycholog- ical theorists-- better than counselors. Nervous and im- patient; not good teachers except for adult or co-in- vestigators, Will accept res- ponsibility from a sense of duty but are easily bored by it. Hate to be distracted from their work. If a woman, will give up work when she marries. Both sexes approach everything from a mental an- gle-- art, literature or the theatre. Lib. Most self-indulgent of the 5 D. Want diversion and es- cape from responsibility, Will make no effort to suc-~ ceed unless interested or greatly benefitted. Should guard against triviality and inconsequentiality. Are eas- ily influenced by their as- sociates, so should choose

them with great care. Need a good example in youth and a training that will instil stability. Are good company and good (temporary) compan- ions, The men often marry for money or security. The more constructive ones are aware of the power and ad- vantage of the 4 in their birthday number. Can succeed in music, writing, art or the theatre, Make good trav- eling salesmen. Scor. Success through con- centration. Good sense of values, good sportsmanship, careful about obligations and dutiful toward loved ones. May inherit money but if dependent upon own ef- forts, are seldom adequate- ly paid. Highly intuitive. Usually attracted to meta- physics and occultism, Want to know reasons and causes, Are good inventors, physi- cians-- often surgeons. or in business. If negative, become selfish and inconsid- erate, "Talent'' for sarcasm. AS ROARS ARS ACASATAY 5 OF SPADES Spades - Labor; Service. or a disaffection with the envi- 5S - Crosses in Labors of ronment or the people that belong Love. to them. They primarily want 5_in S - Opportunity through change for the sake of improve- Change. ment-- not alone for themselves The Mars Card in the Venus L. Displaces the 10 H in the Nat. Sp.

The restlessness inherent in all 5's assumes a different qual- ity in Spades. Here it is not so much a question of dissatisfac- tion, a personal urge for freedom The Cross of the 5 S comes through their affections. They are seldom fickle or disloyal, usually love deeply and perma- nently, It is those they love who bring the anxiety and heart- ache. and friendly cooperation, Gain through foreigners and foreign interests, publica- tion, wide distribution, etc. In the case of a close part- nership, it is best to let the partner take the lead. Business judgment is apt to be uncertain and impractical. Apt to have an agreeable association or connection with hospitals, institutions or places of confinement. In case of illness are usually well cared for. Profit can be made from business connec~ ted with supplies for doctors, nurses, medicines, etc, Bene- fit is likely through connec- tion with governmental inter- ests-- diplomacy, secret ser- vice, bureaus, etc. Strong attraction for the eccult and psychic. Usual- ly have intuitional power and should cultivate it. Are well adapted for dealing with fra- ternities, lodges and orders. Should seek to own property. Not likely to do much trav- eling after 55. The latter years usually peaceful and se- cure; should mot seem monoto- nous-- even to the Aries birth- date-- for there is ample op- portunity to pursue the learn- ing they have lacked and needed.

Merchandising the most pro- fitable. Well adapted for public life or a governmental position. More ambitious for place and power than other 5 S. Interested in economics, government finances and man- agement of civic and commu~ nity projects. Believe in "controls" and regimentation, Personally ambitious but will make sacrifices in private life for a worthy cause. Ruler- Uranus. Averse to routine, Nervous, Broad and tolerant view- point. Have talent for music which should be cultivated; can develop great skill in stringed instruments. Inter- ested in human problems and progress. Can succeed in real estate, Apt to take many jour- neys for business. Want per- sonal freedom. Fine writers. Ruler- Neptune. Prefer art, music and lit- erature to business, but can succeed in oils, perfumes, shipping and distant commerce, Restless but less energetic and determined than other 5 S. Are often philanthropic and universally minded. Sponsor educational projects, move~ ments for the improvement of hospitals, jails, etc. Ruler- Mars.

have faith in the article. If inclined to metaphysics will go far, Sense of drama; are good speakers and convin- cing in arguments. Latent tal- ent for art-- especially dec- oration, furniture, architec- ture. Good taste, good judg- ment. Can write or teach. Ruler- Venus, Home important and necess- ary for happiness-- but want to rule it. Set in opin- ions. Fine writers-- usually in serious vein, essays, phil- osophy, etc, Women often have fine voices; dramatic person- ality. Meet with many changes but dislike them. The Mars Card in the Mars L. Displaces the 4 C in the Nat. Sp.

This is a very stable and de- pendable 6-- affectionate and emo- tional but seldom swept off their feet by their feelings. The dis- placement of the 4 C assures them of a certain amount of mental bal- ance and good judgment-- IF they choose to exercise it. There is a tendency to be ra- ther easy-going and the love of ease, comfort and harmony often interferes with making any mark in life that involves a struggle. They are humanitarian and self- sacrificing, but they sometimes disregard their Mars position and their Club displacement and stay by the fireside instead of brav- ing a cold world. The men are in- clined to be rather more active than the women. All 6 Hearts have come to express and to teach the Law of Love. They know that "love is the fulfilling of the law" (Rom. 10:13) and they also know that they must love in order to be loved-- an important thing for all Hearts.

Through the power of their affections they can overcome all difficulties; when they depart from their law they are unhappy. There can be no “eye for an eye, tooth for a tooth" for these na- tives. They must forgive all in- juries, cancel all debts and still "love the sinner", They are trus~ tees-- not owners-- of their mon- ey. This is the Christmas Card; they are bearers of pifts-- and as a rule they enjoy being Lords and Ladies Bountiful. While always protected, they like to make money themselves and usually have little difficul- ty in so doing. They are frequent- ly found, however, in some chari- table or institutional work where their services are contributed or their salaries negligible. The work they are doing or the pro- jects in which they are interested are more important than the re- turns. They like to live well and they want money at their dis- posal, but usually for the sake of freedom in giving and sharing. They should strive for a good education. They have no difficul- ty in learning but they do not always retain or profit by what they learn.

They dislike changes, Home is important and once the sur- roundings are established, they want to keep them as they are. If called upon to travel, they set up a temporary home as they go-- often loaded down with para- phernalia and familiar gadgets which make them feel at home. Family pictures are always in their pockets, family love in their hearts. Unless loving negatively-- that is, selfishly-- they are comfortable, delightful people to know. The faults to overcome are criticism, domination and a tendency to take charge of the affairs of others and run them in their own way. They are good managers, but should wait to see if their management is wanted.

There may develop an idealistic platonic friendship that adds to happiness. The Law of Love becomes very easy to express and raise to the level of u- niversality. Those of simi- lar interests are drawn to them and great spiritual de- velopment is usually found in these later years-- all the more surely if the goal has been set earlier.

Scor. An easier card for a wom- an than a man. The women may be less concerned with "the world, the flesh and the devel" and often become cos- mic mothers. Both sexes are usually drawn to the occult, ancient mysteries and meta- physical teaching. The feel- ings are intense, the critic- al faculty strong. They should strive for gentleness and tol- orance. Apt to follow heal- ing lines and can succeed in them as a profession, Astronomy, astrology, aero- nautics, archeology -- all that deal with the partial- ly known are means to suc- cess. Thorough investigators. Sag. More self-interested than the other 6 H birthdates; more willing to compromise. Have psychic power. May be interested in two things at once-~ and work at both, If the job is commercial, will turn to metaphysical inter- ests at night. Less depen- dent upon home and family. Should follow inclination to teach or may become in- troverted and neurotic. Can succeed as writers, publish- ers or exporters.

Cap. Scientists, writers and statesmen belong to this card. Are natural philan- thropists. Much concerned with questions of public in- terest, principles of govern- ment, justice and the laws that deal with it. Great sticklers for principle and law. Become pillars of the church, model citizens, etc. More tolerant and broad mind- ed than many. Capricornians. Sponsor established Causes. Faithful and dependable. Clubs ~ Mind 6C ~ Intuition 6 in C - Mental Responsibility. The Saturn Card in the Neptune L. Displaces the 8 S im’ the Nat. Sp. This is a messenger's card-- a John the Baptist who has come to prepare the Way for salvation. There are many 6 C who are una- ware of their important mission, unwilling to take the time or trouble to develop intuition--- which is their greatest gift--- and unable to separate their per-

The men have loy- alty and devotion to family interests but should guard against being dominated by some woman in close relation- ship to them. Both sexes have charm and friendliness. Mar- riage is usually fortunate and happy-- from an emotional angle-- but the strong Saturn emphasis on the card itself is likely to manifest in some form of trial for which nei- ther side may be responsible. In personal relationships they should guard against taking too much for granted. (Note that this card is in the Crown Line).

Success in occupation will come through understanding of human nature and application of innate knowledge. Mental pursuits are best~--education- al, judiciary, political, etc-- or the educational aspect of any given talent-- teaching, lecturing or writing on the subject, Both moral and mate- rial obligations must be thor- oughly discharged. Success in dealing with groups. This is a fraternity card. Finances are protected and affluence often attained. Money can be made through psy~ chic or intuitional gifts, even without any commercial motive. Money should not be allowed to take first place; when it does, the peace of mind is threatened and sel- fishness develops. In all ca- ses of "high calling" this is 4@ grave pitfall. that the "initiate" ele~ ment is strong in these peo~ ple. Karmic relationships and experiences are in their pattern and there should nev~

er be any rebellion against them. What comes must be ac~ cepted as their due, This is often a physician's card, so schooled in impersonality as to seem hard-boiled and un- sympathetic, despite the in- ner urge for healing and ser- vice. Overwork should be a- voided lest the health be broken down and the balance threatened. Old in experi- ence and old in wisdom, they can be of immeasurable ser~ vice to their fellow man. There may be two lines of activity, equally at- tracting, and difficulty in deciding between them. When the decision is made, the one not selected for concen- tration should become a hob- by or avocation; there is u- sually an element of talent or latent ability that should not be wasted. The right se- lection is usually made un- less the desire to stand well with others or the sense of expediency prevails. A ten- dency to show favoritism or cater to those of prominence should be checked.

ed and possibly lonely in the earlier years. This is less evident as life progres- ses. They want companionship, but it must be with those of intelligence and understand- ing. If opportunities for travel have been rare, they are likely to be plentiful after 50. Until their last day on earth they retain an interest in universal condi- tions, higher thought, reli- gion and philosophy and it broadens and increases in old age.
