Have no desire for the lime- light but may be forced into it because of their gifts. Like the other Cancer birth- date, home conditions may in- terfere with their freedom, but not their mission. Absorb their surroundings, so live under great tension-- often affecting the nerves. Should have periods of aloneness to recuperate. A card of inheri- tance-- either by marriage or from their own families. May have periods of financial lim- itation but should never have to look for money. Are loyal, devoted and self-sacrificing. Great awareness of karmic con- ditions and never resent them. Are perfectionists in their work. Talents are diversified but music predominates; re- markable technicians. to serve without recompense and a realization of the ne- cessity for money as a means of discharging obligations. Sense of values uncertain and may experience lack to a great~ er extent than other 6 C. If they become materialistic are bound to lose. Must be servers or philanthropists. Should be at the head of own business. Less mental and can succeed

in commercial lines better than others. Good lawyers, man- ufacturers, bankers, real es- tate operators. Can organize charities, welfare movements, etc. Valuable group members. Want a reason for every- thing they do; want to know exactly what they're dealing with, Inclined to live within themselves. Resent intrusion and criticism from others. Not usually well adapted for marriage; work best alone. Perfectionists in their work and exacting with others. Good statisticians, chart- ists, detail workers. Too im- patient for teaching. Should cultivate faith and their in- tuition; otherwise may be- come selfish and penurious. Give too much power to money. Often seek seclusion through a fear complex. Both sexes are often dom- inated by some woman. To function happily, must have appreciation. Highly emotion- al, lovers of peace; will make many compromises and sacrifices to obtain it. May get into a rut through fail- ure to use their mental gifts, Faithful and affectionate; are messengers of peace, Must strive to live positive- ly and not fear to express themselves. Too modest. Usu- ally high minded and inter- ested in humanitarian pro~ jects, Religiously inclined. Ruler~ Mars.

Strong in will and emo- tions. Intense and dram- atic. Fixed ideas often in- terfere with influence and progress, Can commercialize their talents and want suc- cess. Demand action-~ no ruts. As leader, can work well with groups. Lawyers, actors, wri- ters, inventors, The women want a position of authority. Ruler- Jupiter. Want to make things work-- and pay. Must remain ac- tive and productive. Public spirited, progressive, Best in a mental occupation--wri- ting, teaching, lecturing. Philosophical and broad-mind- ed. Should develop any talent for an emotional outlet; may commercialize it. 6 in D - Adjustment to Values, Completion of Obligations. The Mercury Card in the Jupiter L. Displaces the 9 C in the Nat. Sp.

VALUES being the most import- ant of all things for man to learn and 6 being a number of set- tlement and completion, it is evi- dent that 6 D is the symbol of all others for the balancing of karmic debts. That this can be done in the lifetime of these in- dividuals is shown by the True Place of the card-- the Jupiter position in the Saturn Line. Sat- urn represents the obligation, Jupiter the blessing and reward. The mundane position of the Birth Card is at the head of the Jupit- er Line-- Mercury-~ and we have only to establish the right atti- tude of mind (Mercury) to accept the justice and necessity of the 6 D mission. The acquisition of knowledge is not easy for these people; the displaced 9 C testifies to obsta~ cles and frustrations along ment- al lines. There may be difficul- ty in acquiring an education in early life-- or there may be un- willingness to bother with it on the part of the natives. Money is vital and they want to begin earn- ing before they know where their abilities lie or what sort of a foundation they need to make a success of it.

Saturn represents Obedience; their first step in development is to learn obedience to their own Law. Early training is vital and those who are handicapped by par ents who are unable to administer it must learn the hard way-- by bitter experience and rigid self- discipline. They must learn what justice means; that every debt demands payment and every obli- gation must be discharged; that strict and fearless honesty is their portion and that nothing less ever "pays off". They are continually being tested. Those who have a good mental start pass the tests eas- ily. They are natural mystics and aspirants; they gravitate toward religion and philosophy and know which values are eter- nal and which are not. They know that their situation in life is exactly what it should be-- for it has been earned, for good or ill. They recognize their miss- ion and mean to fulfil it. The others may learn late in life-- but they do learn at last, even though long established hab- it interferes with application.

Finances may go to extremes-~ and remain in one state for a long time-- up or down. In a long period of "down", the unaware ones may succumb to discourage- ment and seek escape in drink or drugs or dissipation. In these cases the fight must be waged a- gainst fear and for faith. A very successful and satis- factory life can be built when money is not made the main object- ive. The women should strive not to depend upon their personalit- ies for their success; the men not to seek a wealthy marriage for the sake of promoting their own objectives. They have a know- ledge of true values and must share and distribute it-- or lose it. Cause and Effect is their Law,

Good earning power is dev- eloped early, but should have a firm foundation. The natural bent should be stud- ied and a choice made that will develop it, There may be a disinclination to trav- el, but it is an aid to bus- iness. There is much innate knowledge but it should be regulated and classified if it is to be retained. Par- ents and home surroundings are highly important. There may be too much domination by a possessive woman whose influence may encourage neg~ ativity. The children of these Birthdates should be instilled with right princi-~ ples and then allowed to act and think for themselves. Make friends easily and gain success through ne~ gotiations with congenial people. Always involved with the opposite sex. Are depen- dent upon love and affection and respond to it. Marriage is often delayed until funds are established. Engagements are apt to be long. Care should be taken not to arouse jealousy or suspicion; these people are subject to it and may become victims of it. Speculation is tabu--but

the temptation is often present. This Card is in the exact center of the Spread--the Jupiter Card in the Jupiter Line. In all act- ivities (Mars) IF the right sense of values is applied, success is certain. By the displacement of the Queen C there is proof that Intuition is the safest and surest guide. Innate fairness makes these people good judges and honest dealers, cess card. 8 also brings ex- pansion and progress and if a good connection can be made with a large organization or a company employing many peo- ple, power is built up by the size and number. There is of- ten a profitable connection with railroads, labor groups and manufacturing, Equal suc~ cess lies in the pursuit of a religious vocation, teach- ing or preaching the laws they have come to uphold.

for these people assail their emotions. Loss, separa- tion or illness of loved ones hit hard when they occur, for love is ardently desired (AH) and the satisfaction it might bring is often denied. Grief may also come through disap- pointment in love-~-disillu- sion or betrayal, and they often become ill as a result, There are instances of 6 D women entering nunneries in an attempt to forget their personal tragedy and lose them- selves in a higher form of love. Harmony is essential to good health and emotional disturb- ance sometimes seriously im- pairs it. These people are good mental (not medical) heal- ers and ardent sponsors and benefactors of the underpriv- ileged. Desire for prominence and importance in groups~~-es- pecially those dealing with la- bor problems. Want to stand well and give the appearance of plenty. Make sure that the returns will be ample for any effort expended. There is usu- ally much correspondence con- nected with these interests, discussion of ideas, messages and a wealth of detail.

Very progressive and influential. Ruler- Venus.

Attract friends and are helped by them. Are loyal and generous, protective and dependable. Stubborn and hard to convince but can be won over through affections. Will work hard for results and for money-~which is usually shar- ed, The birthday number often brings unforeseen trials--es- pecially emotional. Fighters for principle. Not personally ambitious but will not spare themselves in working for a cause or an idea. Are child- ren of nature; can succeed in products of the earth or dealing in commodities. Ruler- Mercury. Versatile and clever. Are skilled musical technici- ans--any occupation, in fact, that requires nimble fingers. Diversified interests. Choose occupations connected with travel or speaking. Can sell by talking. Authorities on data and statistics but sel- dom have time or patience for deep study. Can succeed in commercial lines, but want a quick turnover.

Much concerned with ques- tions affecting social con- ditions, political economy and international problems. Usually traditional or reac- tionary, but may be progress- ive through the birthday num- ber. Very humanitarian. Want universal harmony and peace. Not fighters as a rule but have strong feelings and many of the writers use a strong pen. Interested in commodit- ies for general use, household helps, etc. May be lazy. ses and well advertised ventures; never satisfied with small returns. Large-minded, big-hearted and generous, Want power and publicity. Are good actors, directors and playwrights. Will not stay in any business that does not ex- pand rapidly and pay well. Ruler- Mercury. Intellectual and studious. Feel they can never learn enough so are never satisfied with their knowledge. Want spi- ritual contentment but are skeptical and demand proof. Apt to change type of work through restlessness or a sense of unfulfilment. Can succeed in real estate or as property managers. Composers-~ technical rather than inspi- rational. Statesmen of the in- tellectual type. Teachers of science; writers, critics. Ruler= Venus, Expressive and emotional. Fluent speakers and writ-

ers. Not always stable in af- fections; apt to make experi- ments in love-- usually be- cause they are perfectionists. Dramatic in their approach to art, business or individuals, Lovers of line, beauty and col- or. May become very religious in later life. Can succeed in artistic or dramatic lines but love is always more important than money--or application to their material interests. Are successful in advertising or radio. ies. Many occult and meta- physical students-~ or merely interested in phenomena. Super- stitious if undeveloped. Good lawyers, metaphysicians or med- ical doctors. Intense and dy- namic; never lazy. The serious side of 6 S is often balanced in youth by association with gay and light- hearted people, especially wom- en. The mother is usually a source of happiness or the sis- ter may be kindly and affect- ionate. Social gatherings, gay parties and entertainments may seem important and desirable but they are soon abandoned when maturity is reached. The natural bent of these people is serious and responsible, but they are often given a good start by the very con- tacts they later dismiss as trivial. Education is import- ant and should be encouraged; it is a big stepping stone in later years. The Venus, Mars and Jupiter cards fall in the Crown Line and the Venus influence starts the development of pow- er. If knowledge has not hitherto been considered an asset, the 10 C directs them to seeking it and it becomes a major satisfaction (Venus). Success in this direction is immediate; learning comes eas- KS ily and as new doors are open- ed their own self-confidence develops and they begin to re- alize that they are not the tests. The wise ones regard themselves as custodians--not owners. Lasting friends are There are but four Birth- dates in this group and

the reaction to the 8 D will be different in each case. Being Spades, however, the natural tendency will be to work for their objectives and to make them pay. Here is the indication that this can be done~-especially if money is not the sole aim. For all of these people their power is greatly increased by realiza- tion that they are instru~ ments of Divine Will and that they must keep in touch with their Source. If 8 D is to give them the key to true val- ues, they must keep them- selves in a state of aware- ness for the instruction. Every 6 S owes it to himself to set apart a period each day of his life for contact with his Higher Self. His knowledge must go beyond the realm of the material; reli- gion and philosophy must be- come a vital part of his sci- entific experiments, his art- istic inspirations or his bus- iness methods. This is a card of great res- ponsibility. If a man, he

is the builder of his own good; if a woman, she will seek men of importance and power. Both are leaders in their own spheres; both are in control of their own situations. The neg- ative or unaware will push oth- ers aside for their own success; they will seek domination rath- er than leadership. However they choose to act, there is always power at their disposal -- and the benefits it brings. We see here both good and bad leaders of labor groups; gree- dy in free enterprise or phil- anthropic in distributing its returns. Instinctively they know how to make things pay and how to control their output. *asneo & 103 SuTyI10M ATTeNSQ “szei0[dxa ‘sz0qBonpa ‘si03TIM *auT}NOI 02 pat} aq jou ysnW *supyay Auew ueyj Juepuad -apuy ssa] ‘aAzze1edooD *TeUtT -3710 pue aAT}¥e19 aay *azotd -x@ pue [SABI] 03 QUBA {sseT -3se1 AT[TBUOStag *1aAO PTI0M 3y2 eye OF JUBM *aAT -8sei1301d pug Suytasu0Tg *siey -29TNYy

au, *Teuosied sz quem AT {eaI fay} eA0T ayy pue sao] TESaoA -TUN Jo autzj00p ayy Yyoread of ewoa eAey Ayr *(H y paced -STd) SBAOT 10J artTsap qea13 ST] alay] ‘owos [TIM sjsaQ 3sejee18 |yz a1eyM st sTYy all numbers, the hardest to understand (and to live with) or the most beautiful and rewarding influence in anyone's life. We have seen that it is the center and foundation of the two plans or Spreads in the "Little Book"=- under the rulership of the 7 Planets, built on the plan of 7 rows down, 7 cards across-~ and set in the center of each suit. The 7th Card is the Neptune Card, the 7th row the Neptune row, which may account, in part, for the confusion that arises in our attempts to understand it. Nep- tune is "extra-sensory'"-- and so is 7, As soon as we use it on the level of cold facts, mundane in- terests and physical responsibil- ities, we deprive ourselves of a good half of its power if, in thus using it, we lose sight of its spiritual significance.

7 is called the dominical or "holy" number, the Sabbath of rest and pause to "see that all is good" that is, perfect. It is this re- quirement of perfection that we can't quite live up to, the rest and pause-for-examination to which .we refuse to give the time. We must therefore encounter the con~ fusions, obstacles and possible defeat that Neptune invariably bestows upon those who lack vis- ion and have no concern for the gifts of the spirit. The key is found in the 7th Statement in the Pattern on the Trestleboard-- "Living from that Will, support- ed by its unfailing Wisdom and Understanding, mine is the. Vic- torious Life". As soon as we have learned to live by that Will, Wisdom and understanding, 7 will bring us to Victory. On the Tree of Life the 7th is the Sphere of Victory. For a better understanding of the Divine Plan for the four 7's, note the True Place of each-- HEARTS is the Neptune Card in the Mercury Line. The ideal of love is the right men- tal attitude. CLUBS is the Uranus Card in the Mars Line. In the acquisi- tion of knowledge intui- tion is to be developed and the channel opened for inspiration.

DIAMONDS is the Saturn Card in the Saturn Line-~ a warn- ing to use strict self-dis- cipline in estimating true values. SPADES is the Jupiter Card in the Neptune Line. This is the sure reward of the seeker after the "higher realm" wisdom. 7 is the number of the soul. In silence, peace and self-exam- ination we go toward "perfection". In criticism of others, suspicion, judgment on face-value and sharp practice we create our own obsta- cles and invite our own difficul- ties, There are no "young souls" with a 7 Birth Card. All know that theirs is the quest for Truth.

“dg *3e8N 243 UT H g 243 sooRTdsTq “J snua, 043 UT paeg ArndI1eyW oy -THSTAF 943 UT saToRIsqg - cm ‘ancy - 5778s Finances are usually limit- ed in early life (note the two 7's in proximity). Loss- es are unforeseen and unex- pected demands are made on the resources, It is not safe to take chances or gamble but the inclination is there. In education there are obstacles encountered, in work there is seldom adequate payment, in love there is frustration and expense. Money is spent read- ily-- usually for self, and the desire for escape incites to travel and a vari- ety of experience. The love of home remains, however, and they always want to return to it. To home they usually re- main loyal; it is only in the personal relationships that instability is evident. They want love but the inclination to suspicion and jealousy des- troys it. Once these people find themselves and settle down, love has a chance to come and stay. Sociability and hospitality are natural; they want to live well and en- joy entertaining.

This is the sacrifice card and if there is no inclin- ation in that direction, it will be replaced by self-pity. The attitudes are fixed but they may be strongly influ- enced by some friend or member of the family. Through this influence an inclination toward religion may be devel- oped and they may become mar~ tyrs to their work; they must be convinced, however, that the cause in which they are interested is a worthy one or they won't stay with it. In any suit Jacks can be frivo- lous and time-wasting. Many of the business diffi- culties are caused by a lack of sound educational founda- tion. Some disappointing ex- periments may be made before the best type of business is found. They are most attract~ ed to something that is stim- ulating and adventurous; rou- tine is distasteful and rare- ly contributes to success. Unless poise has been acquired through spiritual development there is much restlessness in this card and the natives need push and incentive in order to follow a given course. Care must be taken to establish friendly relations with employ~ ees; loss and imposition may come from that source,

Any illness-- contracted or congenital-- may be linger- ing and difficult to diagnose. Although interested in chemi- cals and drugs, they are usu- ally averse to taking medi- cines themselves. Their own state of health or the active illness of some member of the family may interfere with bus- iness or personal plans. They like to investigate and exper- iment in cures and prevent- ives-- but not on themselves. The mind is usually keen and there is often an uncanny ab- ility in mathematics. it should always be in close cooperation with some one else-- a person of high intel- ligence preferably. There is a tendency to shift responsi- bility so a careful selection should be made when it comes to any close partnership. Work interests will proceed smoothly if a spirit of har mony and friendship is pre- served. In all dealings an adviser should be on hand.

the Venus L; now in Mars). The desire to become a lead- er or owner seems to increase with the years, but there is always some uncertainty re- garding what course to take. They want to travel and usu- ally leave the place of birth, often establishing a perma- ment residence at a distance. The November birthdate espe- cially is attracted to the sea and may want to own his own ship. The majority want to be near the water. Unless there is some obligation that prevents it, these people of- ten become rovers in the la~ ter years. Must guard against self- indulgence, laziness and selfishness. Want to shine in their chosen work-- as star and benefactor. Like to live in ease and comfort but often have to work hard for success. Love affairs are in- tense while they last. There is much talent in this birth- date and ability to succeed independently if the talent is cultivated and an artist- ic or dramatic line chosen. If they go into business they need cooperation and guidance. Are good talkers and make excellent trial law- yers. Can also engage in ad- vertising, publishing and (in some cases) banking. Ruler- Mars Have fixed ideas-- and devotion to them. Cannot

once formed. Subject to acci- dents that may interfere with their work. May have sudden breaks or climaxes in their lives, long-lasting illness- es or a chronic condition, in- herited or acquired. More du- tiful and self-sacrificing than the other 7 H birthdates, (of- ten needlessly), and seldom ap~ preciated. Given to unconven- tional unions. Must guard a- gainst escape from discour- agement through drink or drugs. May be strongly attracted to occultism and change the whole course of their lives. If so, become active in secret orders. Are seekers for truth. Succeed as teachers, lectur- ers or writers. If not inter- ested in the mysteries are apt to follow engineering, me- chanics-~ or undertaking. Ruler- Jupiter. Intellectual and practical-- but also endowed with imag- ination and a sense of poetry. Nervous and restless-- actual- ly seeking peace and poise. Should seek an occupation in the field of education, liter- ature or philosophy--anything that taxes their fine mental capacity. Clever (often gift- ed) at working in bronze, tin or marble. Money does not come easily and should be budgeted. Should have frequent sojourns in the country-- alone.

Highly ambitious. More self- sufficient than the rest, Dignified and self-important. Critical and easily offended. Serious-minded and responsi- ble. Should never retire; need constant activity. Should av- oid connection with hospitals, institutions of confinement. Can succeed in business or as trustees, government officials or public administrators. 7_in C - Spiritual Power in Rev- elation. This card never lacks the in- spiration of Uranus, as its True Place is also in the Uranus posi- tion (Mars Line). We have the 3 rulerships to consider-- Saturn for the Line, Uranus for the po- sition and Mars for the True Place. Saturn will always make trouble for us if we disregard his laws, and thus it is with the 7 C. Instead of cultivating their great gift of intuition and listening for their instruc- tion, they too often succumb to the Saturn pressure, accept dis- couragement (or rail against the obstacles in their path) and wor- Ty over a multitude of things that never happen.

The displaced 8 D assures them of the power behind them-- a power that not only gives them an opportunity to exercise right judgment of values but secures their protection when discrimin- ation is exercised. The Mars ru- lership of the True Place says, Work for it, fight for it if ne- cessary but USE it at all costs. These people can always make mon- ey (their chief worry) if they will work for it. Many of them do work, but periodically they get into a panic and plunge into some unwise investment or get- rich-quick scheme which leaves them poorer than ever. Much of the trouble lies in a lack of preparation. Being in the realm of Clubs, a thorough education is the prime essential, When this is neglected they get off to a bad start, as far as ac- complishment is concerned. Some periods of financial limitation are bound to come and they need trained minds and well stocked brains to combat them,

As with all 7's, there is a subconscious urge for spiritual de- velopment. The 7 C wants proof and tangibility and when that is not forthcoming they abandon the par- ticular creed or code they have been experimenting with and try another, If their state of mind about religion is sufficiently troubled, they will keep on seek- ing until they find a faith to cling to. The men of this card should strive to work under the K C; in that Sequence the answers to his problems will come more readily. There is sometimes scandal and backbiting connected with this card-- especially for those who waste their time and substance and scatter their energies. Trouble in their work usually comes through labor disputes and unreliable em- ployees, This often reacts unfav- orably on their health, increasing their natural nervousness and men- tal confusion. They must realize that they can succeed-~ and splen~ didly-- through work and applica~ tion. They must shun petty and frivolous associates, psychic ex- periments, gambling, drink and careless, thoughtless living.

ther. Are more at peace when living close to the earth. Should strive to eliminate fear of insecurity. May gain @ position of authority which dispels their fears. May be interested in social and pol- itical and prison reform. Have a more universal view- point than many 7 C and are usually well educated and well balanced. Are studious and philosophical. May attain a high place in government ser- vice. Good writers-- usually along serious lines, ~ history, philosophy, etc.

ple are doubting Thomases. Very talkative and self-ex- pressive by nature, but sel- dom reveal their true senti- ments on any subject. This subtlety may give them an oc- casional advantage in busi- ness but does not help their personal relationships. They like to sound people out and agree to whatever is popular or expedient. Will never com- mit themselves without keep- ing an ace in the hole. Are interested in the races, in business involving transport- ation or anything that moves rapidly and frequently changes. The more developed and educat- ed often go to the other ex- treme and become deep thinkers, philosophers or psychologists. For them writing is the most important field of influence, Those who are less exceptional can succeed as lawyers, agents, salesmen or auctioneers. Ruler- The Moon. but it is rare). Restless and ambitious but not always sure of themselves, They need en- couragement and affection. In

certain directions, however, they have strong opinions and firm convictions and for these they will go to any lengths to uphold. Of all the 7 C they are the most depen- dent upon affection and most devoted to those they love. They want to shine as spon- sors of the underprivileged but their timidity keeps them in the background. Are disturbed about lack of mon- ey and are always charitable with what they have. They are idealistic, poetical and mu~ sical but cannot commercial- ize the arts. They make good managers of apartment houses and hotels, good caterers and successful dealers in household commodities. They often like to follow the sea or live close to it. Can deal in liquids-- especially per- fumes. the other Cancer birthdate. Like the limelight and are attracted to the stage or platform, whether talented or not. Many playwrights and actors are found in this date; also singers, musicians and lecturers. The women are es- pecially gifted in clothes de- signing, running dress shops or executing orders for inter- ior decorations. Like display and are clever in creating it. Work best when they

have an appreciative audi- ence. Influenced by home and family. Usually have a home that is artistic and in good taste. Want cooperation but are unusually independent-- for Cancer. Must avoid sel- fishness and a critical ten- dency. Like to have others dependent upon them. Are gen- erous and protective.

Aug. 19. Ruler- The Sun Leo. Self-confident and highly ambitious. Aspire to a po- sition of authority in what- ever line they follow. When they have learned tolerance and developed a humanitarian spirit, become valuable lead- ers-- but this usually is de- layed until they are advan- ced in years. Must have prom- inence and excitement, so many are attracted to the theatre -- and often make a success of it. Better for the stage then the movies for they pre- fer to be their own directors. If in a commercial line, should be at the head of the business. Strong supporters of law and order. Reactionary. Original and creative. Good merchandisers, customs house brokers or government offi- cials. Always dramatic. Sept. 17.Ruler- Mercury. Vir. Can drive sharp bargains. Should cultivate breadth of viewpoint in money matters and avoid pettiness and penu- riousness. Good tax experts, accountants, technicians. Ef- ficient and thorough in draw- ing up documents, legal pa- pers (briefs) and trust deeds; mever miss a point. Dislike limitation but do not exert themselves to eliminate it. Have periods of generosity and extravagance. Should try to eliminate ulterior motives. Ruler- Venus. Love art, music, poetry-~ and beauty. Strong reli-~ gious inclinations-- not usu- ally orthodox, Like ceremony and "setting" with their reli- gion. Usually endowed with a strong humanitarian urge and will use their talents to pro- mote culture, education and general welfare. Will become actors to “elevate the stage" or marry to reform the mate.

Want money badly but cannot always keep it; self- indulgent spenders. Like to be prominent in the community and appear well-to~do. Will contribute to projects but want their names at the top of the list. Apt to be lazy but will work if there is a chance to accumulate. Are of- ten gifted along artistic lines and should cultivate any latent talent; this brings out the best of them. If make a ca- reer of business, may become ruthless and penurious. In this case money is apt to be a problem, but sacrifice is cheerfully accepted. The usual Aug. 6 people are the most monied of all the 7 D; they are either born to money inherit it or marry it.

In that case, the personal life leaves much to be de- sired. Have many opportuni- ties but seldom embrace them. Will spend lavishly on home and personal comfort. Need to be loved for themselves but are usually sought for their money. When these people be- come truly philanthropic and eager to share their wealth, they stand a fine chance of personal peace and happiness. The usual picture is one of boredom, loneliness-- and an accumulation of ailments. Succeed best in a large busi- ness-- banking, brokerage or manufacturing. May be good lawyers, actors or directors. Ruler- Mercury. Must work hard to attain a key position. Apt to be meticulous and fussy, swamped with detail. The mind is keen but critical; their very men- tality may become a burden. Must avoid worry and fretful- ness. Very successful at re- search and technical details. Also well adapted for real estate, commodities-- especi- ally groceries--merchandising and selling small articles. Can teach or write if other factors in the chart are sup- porting.

This is the most artistic and gifted of all the 7 D. Great painters belong to this birthdate, actors and compo- sers. To the majority, art and beauty come first. Inter- ested in foreign countries and foreign travel. Good foreign agents, diplomats and ambas- sadors. If not artistically inclined, should study law. They feel on the defensive-- partly from a desire to learn quickly, partly from fear that they cannot accomplish all that is expected of them. Are good students and good debat- ers. Throughout life they are "joiners" and they start early. They are subject to sudden in- terruptions or calls in vari- ous directions. They are re- garded as errand boys-- or girls-- and are constantly at the beck and call of others. Are attracted to those of strong mental power. A well 2s educated man-- relative or not -- may have a great influence. The women are apt to marry an older man who is in a mental type of work. Very seldom at- tracted to frivolity. The emo- tions are deep and lasting but emotional relationships are built more on respect and ad- miration than on demonstration. Very capable at handling ac- counts and finances for oth- ers. Absolutely trustworthy. Often found in charitable or hu- manitarian organizations-- Red Cross, Boy Scouts, etc.-- and usually behind the scenes. Are humble and self-effacing but no door-mats. Will work very hard to get things done-~ with or without remuneration. Keep their private lives and business well separated. Legal minds. Interested in living well

ey to make it possible. They seldom have to struggle for a good living unless the early ed- ucation has been much neglected. The pleasure connected with mak- ing money is less materialistic than in most cases; they want than for pride of possession. Benefits come through travel, especially by water. They are good mixers and firm friends. In case of any illness of self or family, they are assured of good care and am- ple funds. Great believers in preserving the health and are strong for preventive and natural methods. Usually live long and remain useful and ac- tive. If they have a health or physical affliction are un- complaining and resigned. They never forget to count their blessings and always feel they are many. in benefits and privileges for employees. Often leaders of welfare groups, better housing committees, etc. Are good exe- cutives. Work well with any- thing connected with the land. Are kindly and diplomatic. Good agents, negotiators, etc. If they have been serious

and much occupied in early life and the middle years, they take more time for sociability when they are older. Life is usually well protected and ve- ry pleasant. They seldom re- tire from active work because the sense of power is increas- ed rather than diminished. Time and money are spent in philanthropic enterprises and they seldom want credit for the gifts and benefits they bestow. If they have failed to overcome their obstacles in the earlier years, they usual- ly can before they die. Some of the early mystery schools taught that 8 was

use by man would bring him evil. From this superstition the prac- tice arose, among many of the old- school numerologists, of assign- ing to 8 the rulership of Saturn as the embodiment of evil. Today we know-- both from experience and from the Higher Teaching-- that 8 is the number of the Sun Our mathematical symbol for infinite measure or "infinity" is the horizontal 8 and this same symbol, as "all power" is over the head of the Magician in the Tarot and of the woman in the 8th card-- Strength-- who is opening the lion's jaws with her hands. When we come to look upon wealth as the symbol of power and write our 8's with a very small loop at the top and a very large one below then, of course, 8 may bring us evil and money may just- ly be called its root. It is actu- ally a picture of God's circle of power above reflected in man's be- low. In the cards we find several geometrical figures of repeated 4's -- two squares, 8 triangles, two tetrads, two right angles, etc-- the security of 4 doubled, or the intimation that upon a firm foun- dation may be built another of greater power and expansion. 8 is the first free number, for it comes

at the "turn" of the 7's. It is the "Eternal Splendor of the Limitless Light". (pattern on the Trestle- board). The natives of these cards can expand in any direction-- ac- cording to their will and the outlines of their pattern. The Bower is always with them. They may turn the power to evil uses if they choose (and there are many 8's who operate very nega- tively indeed) but they are free; they reflect God's Plan but they are not bound by it. HEARTS have the power to harmon- ize their lives, increase their resources and expand their field of service through Love-- personal, universal and Divine. CLUBS have the power to acquire knowledge-- and the obli- gation to disseminate it. If they will not learn and teach they cannot ful- fil their mission. They must follow the admonition of St. Peter to “add know- ledge to your faith". DIAMONDS have the power to acquire wealth-- and the obligation to share it. The "Limitless Substance" is at their dis- posal, twice over. They are "crowned", therefore custo- dians and trustees. They represent the bank of spir- itual and material security

Unless working under the Personality Cards, the early life may be monotonous but is usually well protect- ed and harmonious. The ment- al equipment is good but not in the "prodigy" class. In- sight and intuition are dev- eloped early. They resist changes and like to remain in one place for a long time. There is a slow but steady increase in the desire for knowledge. Can always over- come any obstacles that stand in the way of acquiring an education. Emotional problems and dis- appointments can be over- come through the power in this card-- although some early ex- perience in love may be, for a time, very devastating. If a love or friendship is once formed, they want it to last "forever". The women of these dates want travel-- and usual- ly have it-- but home roots are strong and a home of their own-- not parental-- is much desired. Unless working under the Personality Card, in the case of women there are apt to Uran. be long engagements. Both sexes K S want money that is sufficient and ample security,

and she is much influenced by it-- especially in relations with the opposite sex. All Heart natives like amusement and diversion-- the Q H more than the rest. This can be a very frivolous and flirtatious individual and the power in the card can be hopelessly dis- sipated. If working positively, the women are faithful, mother- ly and warm-hearted and may overdo the mother-love complex. The men may be dominated by the wife or mother, but pre- fer the society of strong- minded women and are often materially helped by them. is in the Crown Line-- prov- ing that the real benefits in these lives come from on high. Power is given to ap- ply what they know-- and if there has been a good educa-~ tional background, much can be accomplished in the ment- al field. There is an in- born sense of true knowledge and mental pursuits are like- ly to be the most lucrative. Their gift of understanding gives them success in deal- ing with others.

The necessity for making money may entail hard work, but the objective is usually accomplished. Possibility of an inheritance or gain of some sort through goods of the dead. Money can be made through healing or a connect~- ion with hospitals or rest homes. There is usually a good health foundation but it may be disrupted through care- lessness. Work best where large numbers of people are employed. Will champion causes that benefit society. Not well adapted for menial work. Good executives. Are found as leaders and dir- ectors of large groups. If drawn to a religious career, make fine missionaries-- med- ical or spiritual-- especial- ly in the foreign field. May be sponsors of religious move- ments or institutions. The Neptune Card in the Mars L. No displacement. This is one of the fixed cards and remains in the same position in both spreads. Neptune holds the key to this card and the Mars "conjunction" either sharpens its power-value or destroys it. The mixture of mental strength, spiritual awareness, psychic power and powerful emotions is sometimes too much for these people to han- dle and dire confusion is the re- sult. When the psychic power is extreme (as in some cases), unless great care is taken for protection, they become fields for possible in- vasion. They should strive at all times for mental balance and not allow their emotions, their fixed ideas or tendency to temperament to dominate them. The charts (as- trological and numerological) of the children of these dates should be carefully studied for any men- tal support along practical lines and these points strengthened in the early years. There is no fi- mer material to be found anywhere than in the 8 C equipment, but it must be guarded and guided.

Set principles and fixed ment- al attitudes have been "brought over" and from these they cannot be swerved. When they are able to clarify their objectives, they al- ways go straight toward them-- and attain them. Through the power of knowledge, which is their heritage, they can overcome any obstacle and attain any desire. Their entire Planetary Sequence is in the Jup- iter line and protection surrounds them in all departments of life. Being a member of the trinity of fixed cards, they are allied with the wisdom of the K S and the sacrifice of the J H. They, too, are on the cross-- as is often ver- ified in their emotional lives. The majority have an avid de- sire for knowledge and allow noth- ing to interfere with its pursuit. They have come to teach and they intend to equip themselves for it. They work better with large groups than with individuals or small cir- cles. The greater numbers seem to generate additional power. If they form any alliance with J H or K S or become attached to any natives of these birthdates, an expansive force is produced that is far-reaching and cannot be broken.

They should adhere strictly to their own birthcard and never attempt to operate under the per- sonality; if they do, they lose much of their power. On the negative side these people may amount to nothing what- ever-- having no awareness of the extent of their influence or the power invested in them. On the destructive side they can, if they choose, reverse this power and be- come black magicians. of themselves, they should avoid psychic phenomena. They are actu- ally in close touch with the high- er planes and have no concern with the astral. Danger lies there-- and the possibility of invasion. Argumentative-- often quar- relsome. Capacity for deep affection-- and need it-- but are undiplomatic and un- intentionally inconsiderate. Impatient with stupidity. Al- ways disturbed emotionally. Succeed best with men-- in politics, law, partnerships, that involve questions of judgment. Very restless and "Martian". May be gifted in art or music but better suit- ed to business.

to stand well with others and are disturbed by inharmony. Often marry for money for the sake of security. Generous if they have the means. Prefer connection with large enter- prises-- manufacturing, bank- ing, produce merchandising. Are often artists or musicians and if they lack talent for success, still want the outlet and take it up as a hobby. Can succeed also in the education- al field and welfare projects. Ruler- Mercury. Interested in people and curious about them. Experi- mental and brilliant-- some- times erratic and wholly irre- sponsible, Natural gamblers because interested in results but seldom win. If willing to cultivate a sense of responsi- bility and conquer the tenden- cy to scatter their forces, can become great teachers, leaders, preachers and arbi- trators. The women like to establish "salons" and bring noted people together. Rulers- Mercury and Moon This birthdate usually falls in Cancer but may occur in the last degree of Gemini. Seldom find them- selves until middle life.

In the early years are unde- cided and vacillating. When settled in a career are eag~ er to impart their knowledge to others. Must exercise self-discipline and culti- vate emotional stability. Can succeed in medicine, nursing or any line that im- proves the physical condi~ tion. Keenly mental and in- tuitive. Women succeed well with home economics, dress shops (or dressmaking) or teaching. In both sexes the gift of writing may be out- standing. Successful in public life and working for humanity. May get into a rut through laziness. Can become leaders in politics or religion, Are patient and make successful teachers of children. Good counselors and judges-- es- pecially in domestic rela- tions. Displaces the Q S in the Nat. Sp.

All eights are power, but it is the 8 D that surpasses the oth- ers in material power. Its posi- tion in the Crown Line not only denotes its high calling but en- dows it with greater freedom of choice than is possessed by any of the cards below that Line. As noted above, (see p. 13) all the Crown Cards are granted Movable Choice and can operate in any po- sition that best suits their re- quirements, This freedom makes the 8 D people very independent-- at times domineering and exacting. The displacement of the Q S only increases this tendency, for Q S$ natives, while willing to work and to serve are always conscious of their position of rulership. The face-arrangement of all 8's denotes balance; again the 8 D surpasses the rest by being in the exact center at the top. The sense of balance is naturally di- rected to values and they usually know when and what to buy and what it is worth-- exactly.

As in all cases, their power is used or misused. They can be philanthropic or mercenary, bene- factors or misers, universally- minded or self-servers. When they choose to operate negatively, the inevitable result is suffering and tragedy. For many the re- quirement is too exacting-- or it may be unrecognized. They may not know that their Birth Card is the Sun Card and that they are direct emissaries of the Central Sun. But the soul knows-~ and if they take the time and trouble to be- come acquainted with their own souls, they will be told. They are the manifestation of the creative principle, closely associated with the resurrection and the certainty of re-birth. Theirs is the gift of sublimation, theirs the ability to overcome all obstacles and conquer all en- emies. The majority are well aw- are of the heavy responsibilities and are willing to discharge them. If this is not realized in youth, recognition comes after 36. As a rule, trust and confidence may be freely given them, They are wise guardians of the goods of the dead and wise conservers of the interests of the living. They will serve honestly and faithful- ly, often at their own expense.

The main pitfalls are love of power (and a certain ruthless- ness in exercising it) and love of the money that enhances it. They may be thrown completely off balance by worshiping the golden calf, forget their obligations, spend the inheritance entrusted to them and swiftly accomplish their own downfall and disgrace. "Those who rise high, fall hard". Their place is already high-- and they have the strength in their wings to keep them there. In any occupation efficiency is stressed. Am- bitious to be the best of their kind. Men succeed as heads of their own business. Often choose merchandising that furnishes supplies for scientists or professionals. A karmic number. Must be strictly just and fair. Not always satisfied in the per- sonal life, Unusual streak of poetry and mysticism for Capricorn. Have high ideals. Ruler- Uranus. Original methods in busi- ness or profession. Full of unusual ideas. Intuitive and inventive. Always mental in attitude. Usually have a strong inclination to reli- gion-- their own brand. Be- lieve in universal good will. If operating negatively, can be cruel and vindictive. Succeed as writers, scient- ists, managers of large en- terprises. Want an unlimited field to work in.

Mystical, idealistic, Are devoted and self-sacrifi- cing. Should avoid being too secretive. Make good confi- dants, investigators, execu- tives in institutions for the sick, afflicted or crim- inal. Can develop psychic gifts to a marked extent. Have a protective attitude toward humanity. Good writ- ers, producers, government officials, Eager to express views and opinions. Want to be out in front as leaders of the people. Should avoid bluster- ing and riding rough shod o- ver those of divergent beliefs. Very curious about public re- actions, Self-promotive and not always able properly to evaluate their mission. Work hard for awhile, then give up. If the interests are universal can become great. Succeed as merchants, public leaders, sur~ geons, publishers.

Not always sure of their ob- jectives so should have them clearly defined and work toward them. Naturally skeptical. Home harmony essential; need support, Must cultivate faith in others. Attracted to stage-- music or acting. Have real religious in- clination, of which they are of- ten unaware. Apt to be influen- ced by personalities in their en- vironment. Should have the cour- age to pursue and adhere to own principles, Self-indulgence and desire for protection makes them weak and useless. Undecided and vacillating be- fore 36. Ability for success but must be allowed to express their ideals or they will go a- long with the easiest way. Per- sonal responsibilities should not be assumed before they have found themselves. Naturally ide- alistic and philanthropic but are blocked by an eye for expe- diency. Fine legal minds. Need people, variety, some travel and enough cooperation to boost their self-confidence, Make good lawyers, journalists, pub- lic officials, counselors. May be too procrastinating for own good. Must stress truth in all dealings.

stantial help from members of the family. They are excellent "re- ceivers". The women especially, but both sexes in general are at~- tracted to those in the profess- ional field. They welcome anything that supports their prestige. Mon- source of many of their tests. Through grief or loss or illness, ally late-- they are forced to learn values. ignored, these people should make every effort to make up for it by joining study groups, going to ones. The emo- tions are so apt to be affect- ed by the desire for money that satisfaction is usually short-lived and a romance that starts out enthusiastically is soon withered if the finan- cial requirements are not met. Interested in travel and tran- sitory contacts. Education is not usually considered as im- portant as experience and di- version until maturity arrives. to do good with it. Self is very apt to come first, but generosity is fundamental and there is usually some important charity or some individual who will benefit at their hands. JO AOTOD ay uo BuTyYeI Jo AqT -Tenb uoaTaweys B aaey atdoad asayy, *Spuaytij 10 saATIBTII JO UOTIENITSs ay UT seBueyo y8no1y perzepuzy 20 pedtay AsyIye e1B saouPjsuNDITD INES include all Numbers and all Planets; 9 is a univer- sal, all-containing symbol. As a Birth Card we may empha~ size the Cross of 5 (the Number of Man) in the venter; we may re- main within the security of the two protective (and often limit- ing) squares at the top and bot- tom or we may leave the lower tri- ad behind us, cross the central path and operate in the higher. 9 is the finisher, for here we reach the end of the single digits; it is also the beginning~-- for we at once proceed to another cycle of 9, which always ends in 9 (18, 27, 36, etc.). It tears down, sweeps away and terminates~~ but it is never lost because it always reproduces itself. It des- troys and it fulfils; it is the end and the beginning.

Because of its destructive and expelling force the ancients allotted it to the planet Mars; because of its all-ness as oppos~ ed to l-ness, it is called the Card of Disappointment. It is ac- tually a Number-- and a Card-- of complete Fulfilment when we grasp its meaning and live its law. The true 9 is dedicated to service-- anywhere, everywhere, to anyone or to everyone. It de- mands the relinquishment of self. But because of its size and ex- tension and inclusion-- an over- all outpouring-- it is the most emotional influence we have to deal with. 9's love more than the rest because they have more "ter- ritory" to cover-- and they suf- fer more; they give more than the rest because they have a greater store-- and it leaves them more bereft; they are more idealistic than the rest because they have been sent to establish Universal Love and the Brotherhood of Man-- and they become more disillusioned.

A Card of Disappointment? As= suredly it is when we chart our lives by personal loves and per- sonal desires. A certain portion of personal love is not denied -- but it always brings trouble. 9 represents the Great Desire-- but the desire must conform to the inexorable law or result in frustration, sorrow and loss. There is no easy personal pattern for any of the 9's, HEARTS have the hardest problem of them all for they live by their emotions-~- and Saturn lays his heavy hand on them; in God's Plan it is the Ven- us Card in the Venus Line-- but God's Plan is universal. CLUBS must seek knowledge for uni- versal good-- never for their sole advantage. When they develop intellectual snobbishness they are doomed to failure-- and isolation. DIAMONDS must acquire-- then "sell all and give to the poor" SPADES must "labor in his neigh- bor's vineyard" before he may gather his own grapes. For all there is sacrifice-- and there is no reward without it. 9's do not sign their names or la- bel their gifts; they are people of "magnificent obsession". Like the Number of their Card, they must lose their identity in order to find their rightful place.

cause financial burdens. Subject to changes in home and associates. Contacts are necessary but should be test~ ed before being accepted as friends. Should never go into business with friends. Sp.

This is an excellent example of the workings of the Law of 9. As in the case of the 9 H the po~ sition is Saturn, indicating a struggle in relation to any men- tal (Clubs) development. The dif- ficulty could be due to finances but since we are in the Venus Line and the Displacement Card is the Q H, it is much more apt to be due to the native's procli- vity for wasting time and emotion- al energy on frivolity and self- indulgence. (The Q H can be a dis- tracting and demoralizing influ- ence). We find the "answer" as well as safe guidance when we note the True Place. Here the 9 C is the Mercury Card in the Jupiter Line-- fortified by the proximity of the 10 C, Expansion (Jup.) for mental (Clubs) development; forget the venusian (Line) satisfactions and hew to the line of duty (Sat- urn) if you would eliminate the disappointments (9) that are sure to follow the negative operation of your Birth Card. God's Plan for you is ruled by Mercury and Jup; by following it you will turn all loss (9) into fulfilment (9).

As a rule, these people have warm and friendly personalities and a keen sense of obligation. They keep promises and pay debts, Note that the True Place of the 9 C is displaced by the 6 D, estab- lishing still another connection with the Birth Card. All 6's are working with karmic law-- and know it-- so there is no desire to escape responsibility Without getting too far out of line as far as duty is con- cerned, the 9 C is an adventur- er's card. They like to gamble and are always willing to take a chance for the satisfaction of curiosity or interest. When the adventurous spirit extends to the field of knowledge, they often be- come extremists-- but at least they are seeking and they are ca~ pable of making some discovery that is of wide benefit. When not influenced by the wrong kind of women, association with one of kindliness and human- itarian qualities increases their own. They worry too much and are too dependent upon approval and sympathy. Many of the early dif- ficulties and gropings disappear in middle life-- or as soon as they are ready to settle down and lay aside the objectives that are strictly personal and emotional.

The women especially must watch the tendency to flirtatious- ness and triviality. The satisfac- tion (if any) is always transito- ry and the chances for a dependa- ble and harmonious association may be spoiled. Any close association formed with 6 D or QH birthdates is an indication that there is a karmic element in the experience gained. All 9's have come to pay off old obligations NOW and in the case of the 9 C these people (6 D and Q H) constitute some of the agents or "collectors". If a commercial line is chosen, can deal success- fully with foreigners and for- eign countries, Strongly at- tracted to travel-- but it may be mental rather than actual. Are intuitive and inspiration- al and can develop extra~sen- sory perception if they work at it. In this regard are al- ways safer in very small se- lected groups or with one de- veloped person. Should avoid public seances. Protection must always be asked for when engaging in this work and it should be left alone until due preparation has been made. good anchorage, work goes well. Interested in labor and politics-- but not in holding any office. Best success in mental lines-- newspapers, in- terviews, personnel managers, etc. Dramatic in manner. Can often succeed in the theatre; showmanship carries them far. Gift for writing-- seriously. Unorthodox; defy established customs. A rare birthdate. Should cultivate balance and

guard against erraticism. Are very intuitive-- on the psy- chic side; temperamental. May become soft and lazy. Should choose work that allows them to serve unless there is some outstanding talent. Make fine dancers if willing to work on technique. Lean to poetry or poetic prose in writing. Must avoid psychic experiments and be sure of their ground. Very self-determined; in- clined to egotism. Make friends easily but are always looking out for #1. Good- natured but have quick tem- pers. Good starters, Must a- void becoming mercenary. Are successful as lawyers (pros- ecutors), politicians, sci- entists. Many are universally minded, progressive and mil- itant for reforms. Work best if there is a heart-interest. Want to live well and have security for the whole family; often have to create it themselves and not depend upon the mate. May write but are best adapt- ed for artistic work-- paint- ing, singing, dramatics, mod- eling, composing. Excellent designers and decorators. Ruler- Mercury. Dissatisfied with results, Impatient, changeable.

Give up too easily. Always have a mental approach. Good draughtsmen, architects, cri- tics, journalists. Opinionat- ed. Skilful with hands and with words. Speakers, report- ers, translators. Good lin- guists. Attracted to theatre, radio, electricity. Always make themselves heard and felt in any crowd. the other Gem. birthdate; more public-spirited. If they remain impersonal, can make considerable money. Want a home but not always happily married. Good guides or trav- el agents, canvassers, stew- ards, demonstrators, etc. Good entertainers, hosts and companions. gD - Dissatisfaction in Money 9 in D ~- Generosity-- or Sel- fishness The Mars Card in the Uranus Line Displaces the Q D in the Nat. Sp. There seems to be no middle ground for these people; they are either philanthropic, generous and protective toward others or live for themselves alone, unable to acquire the money they want and dissatisfied with life. For all 9's, there is no escaping obsta- cles and disappointment if they are selfish. The True Place is the Neptune Card in the Saturn Line-- which is but another way of saying that duty, responsibil- ity and full payment constitute the goal in life and should be idealized and sought eagerly. When it is, it is attained happily. There are no weaklings in the birthdates of this Card-- but in early life or in the case of some strong attachment, there is a ten- dency to be dominated. When this occurs, once the tie is broken, they may become domineering them- selves-~ to "get even''. There is usually a goodly amount of self- insistence and aggressiveness. They want to think and act for themselves-- and should-- but fear they may not be able to un- less they demand it.

There is a close tie between the 9 D and the Q D-- a tie that surpasses the displacement influ- ence. Those belonging to these dates have some karmic debt to work out-- and the association is not always an agreeable one. In the case of a woman, there is a "double dose" to be reckoned with and great care should be ta- ken in handling these associa- tions. For the most part, these peo- ple are slated for success. They are inclined to materiality-- and may be mercenary, but the natural tendency is toward generosity, even though they never forget to protect themselves. If they are to avoid disap- pointment, they must clearly de- fine their values-- and they should be helped to this end very early in life. "As the twig is bent" is very significant in their case and colors their entire life. Those whose inclinations are on the spiritual level and have ac- quired a working philosophy have few obstacles to overcome. Con~ sciously or unconsciously they follow the Law of 9 and GIVE of themselves, their efforts and their goods. With any 9, a cer- tain degree of "cosmic urge" is a requirement. Those who deny it only defeat themselves.

The women have more to over- come than the men for they are al- ways struggling with their person- ality-- and that QD is difficult. The men are apt to marry women who cause them worry, cost them money or fail to give satisfac~ tion. Both sexes are apt to over~ estimate their ability. Both are interested in things that move too fast and are therefore sub~ ject to accidents. They often choose~- and are well adapted for-- welfare work, Red Cross, Salvation Army, YMCA, or some allied branch of public service. The less they concentrate on their own security, the better.

Ambitious and studious. Approach everything from a mental angle. Orthodox and cautious; take no chances, For Capricorn-- quite univer- sally minded. No imagination. to argue and often take the opposite side-- not from con~ viction but because they en- joy it. Interested in people and the rights of the under- dog. More biased than bal- anced; go "all out" for any cause they espouse. Imagina- tion, enthusiasm and emotion- alism carries many into the theatre-- often to marked suc- cess. Can also succeed as cor- respondents, columnists or government officials. Very talented in art or music (also dancing) or very scientific, capable of making a valuable contribu- tion to general knowledge and progress. Idealistic and hu- manitarian, In the less gift- ed and intellectual there is a tendency to drift, Benefit- ted by interests at a dist- ance. Make good agents, man- agers of transportation, hos- pital or prison employees, secret service investigators. May gain through perfumes, oil, narcotics or fishing. Promoters, politicians, members of armed forces, orators and propagandists. May also succeed in publish- ing. The majority are not attracted to merchandising. Always self-promotive-- often too aggressive and egotistic. Good talkers, good salesmen, and like a good fight. Feel very sure of themselves, Ruler- Venus

and clubs but want some of- ficial position near the if allowed to keep their in- dependence. Honest and reli- able; affectionate and pro- tective. Attracted to intel- ligent women. Can commercial- ize products for general use. Good cooks and gardeners. Ruler- Mercury. Mental and inquiring. Are highly nervous-- which af- fects the health. Usually want more power, authority and money than they can han- dle or acquire. Apt to devel- op chronic conditions due to negative states of mind and a tendency to hypochrondria,. Good traders, writers, talk- ers, extemporaneous speakers. Illnesses and obstacles of- ten come unexpectedly; sur- prising changes. Should be careful of lungs. Patriots and sticklers for principle. Sincere devo- tees of worthy causes, Objec~ tives often delayed until la- ter years. Good government leaders and officials. When merchandising is chosen, usu- ally deal in small articles, home products, foods. Not an easy birthdate; life full of effort and hard work.

Can be helpful and val- uable but should not be al- lowed to impose domination. The father or older brother may exert too strong an in- fluence and become restrict- ive. The women may marry ol- der men-- protective but paternal-~ and may chafe un- der the relationship. Occupation is involved with monied people or en-~ tails handling considerable sums. If a connection can be formed with those of means

or who are dealing in min- erals, many of the business difficulties of the 9 S will be mitigated. If these asso- ciations are formed, there is always an element of uncer- tainty in them. Liable to breaks and displacements. The greatest benefits come a chosen course adhered to. Security lies in established conditions which provide a steady, ample income rather than affluence. Work flour~ ishes more freely when based on friendly relations, Work and application should be steady but not allowed to produce a rut.

and a spirit of discontent are bound to interfere with it. These people can become mental healers and are them- selves responsive to mental therapy. When well balanced, have a soothing and curative effect on the mentally dis- turbed. May have a skeptical attitude of mind-- which should be overcome; inwardly know better than to doubt. Fear of financial inse- curity interferes with their work and the "direct contact" they are well able to establish. Should never have to worry about money (and need not) unless they are deliberately wasteful. Work with more contentment and freedom if associated with some one who will at- tend to the details (and ex- penditures). Can make money from real estate and should own their own homes. liar ideas-- sometimes considered erratic. High id- eals, but have difficulty in realizing them. This is a neo~ phyte's card; debts must be paid before any further prog- ress can be made-- and there is a great desire for prog- ress along spiritual lines, Worry and material concern get in the way' once these are overcome, "illunination" is assured. Travel is apt to be disappointing. Emotional ties become less important in the later years.

TENS are called "Success" Cards-- a term that seems far too weak to apply to such a powerful symbol. To the Pythagoreans 10 represented Deity, Heaven, Eter- nity and the Sun*. To ourselves 10 is Man (1) and "God Ahead"(0). -- a heaven to reach for, though we may not grasp it. 9, with all the numbers be- hind it, is the completed cycle of man's experience; 10 begins the new cycle, on a higher spiral, the cycle of man's Aspiration, On the tree of Life there are 10 Spheres or "10 Emanations of the Divine Sephiroth" and Malkuth, the Kingdom is the 10th-- our own world; in the Pattern on the Tres~- tleboard there are 10 Statements; our laws of conduct were given in 10 Commandments and the Decimal system is used all over the world. Unattainable as it may appear and "mighty'"' as it surely is, 10 is ur number to use and to "succeed" with. If 10 is your Birth Card you have the tools with which to “fully accomplish".

In the Mundane Spread we have placed one of the 10's in the ex- act center (it would be diamonds!); another stands behind it; a third is the center of the Mercury Line and the fourth leads the Sun or "Crown" Line. In any position in which the card is held, we find the triangle-- symbol of perfect- ion in expression. Here is success on all sides, as man stands in the center-- two patterns of 5 (the number of man), carrying the protection of the pentagram. Every 10 should consciously live in the realization that he is Man twice over-- Man as himself and Man in the image of God. So are we all-- but 10 is the only card that has that 0 ever beside it. No forward step can be taken without meeting it-- face to face.

Since What Constitutes Suc- cess is a purely individual ques- tion, we find every variety of expression in these 10's-- a few of the very great, more of the very successful and the vast ma- jority who are utterly oblivious to the power in the equipment they have brought to earth as their kit of tools. None of them can be wholly devoid of elements of greatness but many can and do live as though the purpose of life were to amass, attain and control. But not even the atheist escapes his divine origin, HEARTS attain their success and overcome their obstacles through the power of Love. CLUBS find their success in the mental field and secure it by sharing their know- ledge. DIAMONDS succeed as Masters of Mat- ter. Their dominion is over “all created things". SPADES success depends upon the degree of maturity they have reached. For them, 10 is their intiation into a higher Grade,

gious. The less serious mem- bers of these dates spend much time entertaining and making a success of running their homes. They take pride in good organ- ization and efficiency and en- joy impressing others with the domestic greased wheels, They prefer to surround themselves with people of high intelli- gence and interesting ideas; if they are "important" as well, they are so much the more welcome. Want to stand well and be popular-- especially with the opposite sex. May be too much influenced by the finan- cial standing of their friends. Are good hosts and good mixers, never at their best when alone. There is always a sense of leadership and initiative and they want their ideas and deci- sions unquestioned. They think they know best~-- and they of- ten do-- but if their poli- cies are voted down, they will gracefully step aside rather than take second place, good reputation must be pres~- erved at all costs and faults, lacks and family skeletons are carefully concealed, They are

fine custodians of secrets-- others' as well as their own. If there is a temporary finan- cial shortage, the world sel- dom hears of it; they manage to cover it up until they are again on their money feet-- having made great sacrifices to maintain "business as us- ual", age (or any other partner- ship) contracted for the sake of money, trouble catches up with them in some form or oth- er, Their life may be inter- fered with by some sick or nag~ ging female member of the fam- ily or by a dependent who may make unreasonable demands. The women may become success~ ful physicians or important nurses or executives in a doc- tor's office, They do not lack sympathy but want to be fully paid for their services.

Displaces the J S in the Nat. Sp.

As has been previously stated, the Crown or "Sun" Cards have the privilege of choosing their posi- tion-- but do not depart from the inherent qualities of suit or num- ber. 10 C, being the first card in its line, is under the rulership of Mercury and is therefore condi- tioned by all that planet stands for-- mental processes, approaches and reactions; ideas and communi- cations; education and mind-train- ing. Being Clubs, it symbolizes knowledge-- its acquisition and dissemination. Regardless of the position under which it may "choose" to operate-~ together with an addi- tional planetary influence-- its basic "ingredients" remain. The displacement of the JS suggests the danger of fixation. The firmmess in the J S and adhe~ rence to principle are well worthy of emulation, but 10 C can go as~ tray through stubborness.

Their greatest success inva- triably lies in the mental field. If their knowledge is applied from high motives, their needs are sup- plied from the "Limitless Sub- stance"; there is seldom any real lack of supply although they of- ten fear limitation. They are look- ing toward the 8 D in their own Line and they should have no trou~ ble in establishing a right sense of values. Again looking forward, the K S gives power through wisdom. The teachers and initiates of these dates may "choose" the 8 D as their Jupiter Card and can make it effective in that posi- tion if they are reaching out for attainment in terms of the soul. If they choose to work with a wider pattern, the A C becomes the Mercury Card and the arrange- ment reads as follows: In this case, however, they may complicate the emotional life by their close proximity to the uncertain 3 H. In the educational field there would be no problems.

There is always the power to make money-- but the effort is sometimes lacking. The gift of choice may become a source of con- fusion for they can't always tell just where they are or should be operating. They want to be pro- ductive and successful but if they have not reached the stage in their development where they can depend upon intuitional guidance; they sometimes waste many valuable years in experimentation before they find the right vocation. "Make your calling and election sure" said St. Paul-- and he must have been talking directly to the 10 Cc. The True Place is the Venus Card in the Jupiter Line. The gift of Knowledge is not the only star in their crown; in the Plan, Love shines beside it with equal bril- liance-- love of their work and love for those with whom they have come to share their knowledge. Those who are working in that con- sciousness are Jupiter's own heirs. ive. May become leaders through willingness to res~ pond to the demands made up~ on them. Must be kings in their own homes. Like to be entertained and waited upon. Attracted to the stage-- as actors. If business is cho- sen, want it big. Fair and honest in all dealings, Emo- tional and warm-hearted. Can write fluently and dramati- cally. If in a government po- sition, more fatherly than dictatorial. Rulers- Venus or Mercury. Those who still carry the stamp of Taurus are in- clined to acting, composing and playwriting and can eas- ily commercialize their tal- ents. The Gemini-born are more mental than emotional or dramatic. These may succeed in the educational field, as eritics or satirical writers, essayists, etc. All are affec- ted by home conditions; in- harmony interferes with abil- ity to concentrate-- and con- sequent success. Deep inter- est (Tau.) or curiosity (Gem.) in metaphysics.

Writers, lecturers, poli- ticians. Should be before the public. Clever and versa- tile. Willing to give service if interested in the "cause". Like to deal with people and changing conditions. Always curious about what makes peo- ple (or things) "tick". Have ups and downs in finances, Often undertake more than they can carry through. Want business that is quick-mov- ing, bringing quick returns. Liable to become over-extend- ed. This is the most important of the money cards for it is in the exact center of the Mundane Spread. Man chose to make money the pivot~ al point around which all else should swing and selected the most powerful of the money cards to guarantee "success". 10 D is one of the Master Cards. In the Divine Plan Receptivity and Intelligence is the focal point-- and while these qualities are relegated to second place in the eyes of the world, the aware ones may "borrow" them by virtue of displacement. The majority of these natives are money conscious but there are many who are, instead, yalue con- scious-~ a very different matter. They translate their "full accom- plishment" into cosmic terms. In general, there is a lack of true spirituality. The type of religion they profess is tradition- al and orthodox; the goal they prize is Acquisition. They are in great danger of becoming mercenary. This is a better card for a man than a woman; the men can ay~ oid the Q D and its unfortunate sequence. For both sexes, there is usually the promise of inheritance. They can always succeed and are al- ways protected where money is in- volved. They may get down to the last dollar, threatened with loss

of business or property-- and then, by clever manipulation or last-minute help, turn the threat- ened failure to success. If a man, will benefit by asso- ciation with a successful woman; if a woman, is likely to be in business for herself or in a key position of management. They all like to associate with people who have something to offer-~ if not actual money, then position or so- cial standing or family back- ground. They are not necessarily snobs but they want to stand well in the eyes of the world and they want their surroundings to give evidence of prosperity. Commercializing their abili- ties comes easily. If they engage in business, they never fear size or extent; they know they can han- dle it-- and make it pay. The True Place is the Mercury Card in the Uranus Line. There are few who lack keen brains and sharp wits and while their education is sought primarily along business lines, they have great respect for knowledge and want to acquire en- ough of it to be able to take their place in any company.

There are many philanthropists and benefactors in these birth- dates, many with a strong sense of universal obligation and a de- sire to put their money to construc- tive use. They are invariably gen- erous with any surplus-- and some there are who will deprive them- selves to help the less fortunate. Great buildings, enterprises and monuments have been established in their name and many good deeds live after them. They are tested by their sense of values. *AYFTBSISATUN |IBATI[ND prnoys *$,0T 243 TI® JO *,U8Ter05,, BuyyzAue Fo [eoTIdeys say *uoTBuswoUTaI IO --usT[ENIT -1tds ‘Aqq[eq10mmy Jo ains jou fuoyZyTTe1 pue setydoso,tyd usa -388] 03 asieAy *[aeqM Bupr20qs 9y3 uo spuey atey3 deay pur emoy 38 ABqs 03 Jezoad fszeak 1a3BT ey ut AT TeToedse ‘{a ABI] YNOGP UTeRIE0Un *soUeIsTP B® 4B ssautsnq 10 atdoad ‘s8uTy uy peqysazaquT JON "saqey

Very patriotic. Excellent government officials-- am- bassadors, representatives, diplomats. Interested in so- ciological questions, state or federal laws, government procedure. If in business, usually for themselves. Self- confident in the business world-- not in their emotion- al lives; changeable in feel- ings. Want to be associated with things that are well es- tablished; no gamblers. Can succeed in oil, mining and manufacturing. in this birthdate. Always have a large following; their works have a wide distribu- tion. Personal lives disturb- ed by doubt and unrest. Make good salespeople, attorneys and realtors. Interested in labor problems. Want spirit- ual security but do not know how to cultivate it. Should make a determined study of metaphysics, the other 10 D. Full of fears and worries. Build their world around their own small circle. Give willing service-- but always person- al. Can succeed in transpor- tation; like travel but sel- dom satisfied with journeys. Good musicians; may be good dancers. Dutiful and devoted nurses. Should serve in broad fields-- impersonally. Lives often narrow and unproductive,

Square-dealing; honest, Nervous and emotional. Ambitious; dominated by a desire to stand well. Will- ing to serve, but want full payment. Need plenty of ac- tion to be happy. Not con- tent with small or inactive business. Can succeed at law but should not become judges; too emotional and biased. Swayed by personal opinions and fixed ideas. Very moral, Should avoid intolerance. Ruler- Venus. Want harmony at home and among friends. Want to stand well and put up a good front. Combine business with social activities, Ruled by emotions and ambition. Apply their efforts to accumulation. Make good doctors; have good “bedside manners". Can succeed in real estate, home furnish- ings, food. Not always as uni- versally-minded as their birth- day number requires. Climbers. Ruler- Mercury Want quick turnovers. Will work only if interested.

Can lecture, teach-- and make money at it. Can overcome the tendency to superficiality by cultivating the mind. Have a variety of skills. Give the impression of instability but are often serious-- even de~ pressed. Uncertain of right place; will make many experi- ments before settling down. Can sell if they have faith in the product, A mental occu- pation best for success. Ruler~ The Moon. Seek important and success- ful people as "background". Self-indulgent. Dislike too much work or effort. Expect help from others. Can be pub- lic-spirited and philanthrop- ic. Writers, philosophers, chants. Like a "social" type Vir.
